title: Migrate Tessie setup and coordinator to tesla_fleet_api
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Proposed change
Migrate the Tessie setup and coordinator core path onto `tesla_fleet_api.tessie` while keeping the temporary compatibility runner in place for unmigrated platforms. Setup now creates one `Tessie` client, fetches vehicles through `list_vehicles`, and stores per-VIN `Vehicle` handles on runtime data. The vehicle coordinator now refreshes through `Vehicle.state(use_cache=True)`, and the Tessie setup/coordinator fixtures were updated to mock the new library entry points.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the Tessie integration's setup and coordinator components to use the new `tesla_fleet_api.tessie` module instead of the deprecated `tessie_api` library. The migration updates the core initialization logic, coordinator state fetching, and test fixtures while maintaining backward compatibility by keeping both libraries as dependencies.

**Changes:**
- Updates setup to create a `Tessie` instance and fetch vehicles using `list_vehicles()`, then create per-VIN `Vehicle` instances stored in the runtime data
- Refactors the state coordinator to use `Vehicle.state(use_cache=True)` instead of the old standalone `get_state()` function
- Adds the new `Vehicle` API reference to the `TessieVehicleData` model for future entity migrations
- Updates test fixtures to mock the new library's class methods instead of module-level functions

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/components/tessie/conftest.py | Updates mock patches to target `tesla_fleet_api.tessie` classes instead of old module functions |
| homeassistant/components/tessie/models.py | Imports `Vehicle` class and adds `api` field to `TessieVehicleData` model |
| homeassistant/components/tessie/coordinator.py | Updates `TessieStateUpdateCoordinator` to accept and use `Vehicle` instance for state fetching |
| homeassistant/components/tessie/__init__.py | Refactors setup to create `Tessie` client, fetch vehicles, create vehicle instances, and pass them to coordinators |

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `2b4c476eab`

[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

Rename Tessie optional API handle test

title: Add keyboard text input services to Apple TV integration
body:
## Proposed change

Adds three new services to the Apple TV integration that expose pyatv's `Keyboard` interface for text input:

- `apple_tv.set_keyboard_text` — Sets text in the currently focused text input field
- `apple_tv.append_keyboard_text` — Appends text to the currently focused text input field
- `apple_tv.clear_keyboard_text` — Clears the currently focused text input field

### Credit

This work builds on the original implementation by @toscano in #152398. Their PR introduced the concept and initial code. This PR picks up that stalled work and addresses all maintainer feedback from @joostlek:
- Service handlers in a dedicated `services.py` file (Mealie/Overseerr pattern)
- Uses `config_entry_id` targeting instead of `entity_id`
- Follows the [action-setup quality scale rule](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/integration-quality-scale/rules/action-setup)
- Rebased on current `dev` (no merge conflicts)

The keyboard focus binary sensor (#152397, also by @toscano) is already merged — this PR adds the corresponding service actions.

All three services validate that:
- The device is connected (`HomeAssistantError` if not)
- The keyboard interface is supported (`ServiceValidationError` with `NotSupportedError`)
- A text input field is currently focused (`ServiceValidationError` if unfocused)

## Type of change

- [x] New feature (which adds functionality to an existing integration)

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue: #152398
- Link to documentation pull request:

It seems you haven't yet signed a CLA. Please do so [here](https://home-assistant.io/developers/cla_sign_start/?pr=home-assistant/core#165638).

Once you do that we will be able to review and accept this pull request.

Thanks!

## Pull request overview

Adds keyboard text input services to the Apple TV integration, exposing pyatv’s `Keyboard` interface to set/append/clear text in the currently focused tvOS text field.

**Changes:**
- Register three new Apple TV services (`set_keyboard_text`, `append_keyboard_text`, `clear_keyboard_text`) and wire them to pyatv keyboard calls with focus/connection validation.
- Add translations and icons for the new services/exceptions.
- Add unit tests covering success paths and key error conditions.

| `homeassistant/components/apple_tv/services.py` | Implements the three keyboard text services and validation/error mapping. |
| `homeassistant/components/apple_tv/services.yaml` | Declares service selectors/fields for UI/service editor. |
| `homeassistant/components/apple_tv/strings.json` | Adds service text + translatable exception messages used by service errors. |
| `homeassistant/components/apple_tv/icons.json` | Adds icons for the new services in the UI. |
| `tests/components/apple_tv/test_services.py` | Adds tests for the new services (happy path + validation/error scenarios). |
</details>

**Changes:**
- Introduces `set_keyboard_text`, `append_keyboard_text`, and `clear_keyboard_text` services with validation and translated errors.
- Registers the new services via an `async_setup` + dedicated `services.py` module.
- Adds service selectors/translations/icons plus a new test module covering success and key failure paths.

commits.message:
Add keyboard text input services to Apple TV integration

Adds three new services to the Apple TV integration that expose
pyatv's Keyboard interface for text input:

- apple_tv.set_keyboard_text: Sets text in the focused input field
- apple_tv.append_keyboard_text: Appends text to the focused input field
- apple_tv.clear_keyboard_text: Clears the focused input field

Services validate that the device is connected and a text input field
is focused before sending commands. Follows the services.py pattern
per maintainer feedback on #152398.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address review feedback: type annotations, specific exceptions, proper test fixtures

- Add proper type annotations throughout services.py (strict typing)
- Use pyatv-specific exceptions (NotSupportedError, ProtocolError) instead of broad Exception
- Use MockConfigEntry from tests.common instead of manipulating internals
- Add test cases for keyboard_not_available and keyboard_error paths
- Fix import ordering to pass ruff linting
- Use TYPE_CHECKING guard for AppleTVManager import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix test isolation: use patch.object for property mock

Replaces class-level property mutation with patch.object/PropertyMock
to prevent test state leakage between test cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address review: use string references and proper integration setup in tests

- Replace duplicated strings in strings.json with [%key:...] references
- Rewrite test fixtures to set up the integration through HA's normal
  setup path with mocked pyatv scan/connect instead of manual wiring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix formatting: sort JSON keys, parenthesized context managers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address Copilot review: return AppleTVInterface directly, revert config schema change

Refactor _get_manager to _get_atv, returning the AppleTVInterface
directly instead of the manager. This eliminates all type: ignore
suppressions since mypy can now see the return is non-None.

Revert config_entry_only_config_schema change as it's out of scope
for this PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore config_entry_only_config_schema per Copilot suggestion

The CONFIG_SCHEMA was added in this PR, so using the correct
config-entry-only variant is in scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix invalid translation references for config_entry_id field name

Replace non-existent common::config_flow::data::config_entry reference
with a plain string in the canonical entry and component-level references
in the others.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix test failures: use AsyncMock for atv, add create=True for patch

- Change mock_atv from MagicMock to AsyncMock so async methods like
  apps.app_list() return coroutines during teardown
- Add create=True to patch.object for text_focus_state since Python
  3.14 is stricter about attribute existence on mock classes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR extends the `person` entity to expose the `in_zones` attribute (recently added to `device_tracker`), allowing automations to inspect the zone membership list directly on persons.

**Changes:**
- Add `in_zones` storage/propagation to the `Person` entity state attributes.
- Reset `in_zones` to an empty list when the person has no valid tracker state.
- Update person component tests to assert `in_zones` behavior for GPS/BLE tracker scenarios.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/person/__init__.py` | Stores and publishes `ATTR_IN_ZONES` on person entities based on the selected coordinates/source state. |
| `tests/components/person/test_init.py` | Extends existing person tests to include assertions for `ATTR_IN_ZONES` in relevant scenarios. |

commits.message: Propagate the in_zones attribute from device trackers in person entities

title: Migrate peco to use runtime_data
body:
## Proposed change

Migrate the `peco` integration to use `runtime_data` instead of `hass.data[DOMAIN]`.

- Added `PecoRuntimeData` dataclass and `PecoConfigEntry` type alias in `coordinator.py`
- Updated `__init__.py` to store data in `entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data[DOMAIN]`, removing the need for manual cleanup in `async_unload_entry`
- Updated `sensor.py` and `binary_sensor.py` to read from `config_entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data[DOMAIN]`
- Updated coordinators to use `PecoConfigEntry` typed config entry
- Removed `assert hass.data[DOMAIN]` from tests since `hass.data` is no longer used

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Migrates the `peco` Home Assistant integration from `hass.data[DOMAIN]` storage to `ConfigEntry.runtime_data` to align with current integration patterns and remove manual cleanup needs on unload.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `PecoRuntimeData` and a typed `PecoConfigEntry` to centralize runtime objects on the config entry.
- Updates integration setup/unload and entity platforms to read coordinators from `entry.runtime_data`.
- Adjusts tests to stop asserting `hass.data[DOMAIN]` usage.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/peco/__init__.py | Stores coordinators in `entry.runtime_data` and simplifies unload logic. |
| homeassistant/components/peco/coordinator.py | Adds runtime data container + typed config entry alias; updates coordinator typing. |
| homeassistant/components/peco/sensor.py | Reads outage coordinator from `config_entry.runtime_data`. |
| homeassistant/components/peco/binary_sensor.py | Reads smart meter coordinator from `config_entry.runtime_data`. |
| tests/components/peco/test_init.py | Removes assertion tied to `hass.data[DOMAIN]`. |
| tests/components/peco/test_sensor.py | Removes assertion tied to `hass.data[DOMAIN]`. |
</details>



<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**homeassistant/components/peco/binary_sensor.py:31**
* Use the CONF_PHONE_NUMBER constant when reading the phone number from config_entry.data to avoid duplicating the raw key and drifting from the rest of the integration (e.g., __init__.py uses CONF_PHONE_NUMBER).
```
    async_add_entities(
        [PecoBinarySensor(coordinator, phone_number=config_entry.data["phone_number"])]
    )
```
</details>

commits.message:
Migrate peco to use runtime_data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Migrate prusalink to use runtime_data

Migrate the prusalink integration from using `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry.entry_id]` to `entry.runtime_data` for storing coordinator data.

## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the PrusaLink integration’s per-entry coordinator storage from `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry_id]` to `ConfigEntry.runtime_data`, aligning it with modern Home Assistant integration patterns.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `PrusaLinkConfigEntry` as a typed `ConfigEntry` alias for the integration’s runtime data.
- Stores the coordinators dict on `entry.runtime_data` during setup and relies on core cleanup on unload.
- Updates platform setup code (camera/sensor/binary_sensor/button) to read coordinators from `entry.runtime_data`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/prusalink/__init__.py | Writes coordinators to `entry.runtime_data` and simplifies unload logic. |
| homeassistant/components/prusalink/coordinator.py | Adds `PrusaLinkConfigEntry` alias and tightens coordinator typing to use it. |
| homeassistant/components/prusalink/camera.py | Reads the job coordinator from `entry.runtime_data` and updates typing accordingly. |
| homeassistant/components/prusalink/sensor.py | Switches coordinator access to `entry.runtime_data` and updates entry typing. |
| homeassistant/components/prusalink/binary_sensor.py | Switches coordinator access to `entry.runtime_data` and updates entry typing. |
| homeassistant/components/prusalink/button.py | Switches coordinator access to `entry.runtime_data` (including in `async_press`) and updates entry typing. |
</details>

Migrate prusalink to use runtime_data

## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the Prosegur integration from storing its authenticated client in `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry_id]` to using `ConfigEntry.runtime_data` (typed via `ProsegurConfigEntry`), aligning it with modern Home Assistant integration patterns and simplifying lifecycle management.

**Changes:**
- Store the `pyprosegur.Auth` instance on `entry.runtime_data` during setup, and rely on core to clear it on unload.
- Update platforms and diagnostics to read the auth client from `entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data`.
- Adjust tests to import `DOMAIN` from `homeassistant.components.prosegur.const` (since it’s no longer re-exported from the package root).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/prosegur/__init__.py` | Introduces `ProsegurConfigEntry` and migrates setup/unload to use `entry.runtime_data`. |
| `homeassistant/components/prosegur/alarm_control_panel.py` | Uses `ProsegurConfigEntry` and `entry.runtime_data` for auth access. |
| `homeassistant/components/prosegur/camera.py` | Uses `ProsegurConfigEntry` and `entry.runtime_data` for auth access and image service calls. |
| `homeassistant/components/prosegur/diagnostics.py` | Fetches installation/activity using `entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data`. |
| `tests/components/prosegur/conftest.py` | Updates `DOMAIN` import to come from `prosegur.const`. |
</details>

commits.message:
Migrate prosegur to use runtime_data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Add new state attribute in_zones to device_tracker
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
Add new state attribute `in_zones` to `device_tracker`

The `in_zones` state attribute is a list of zone entity_ids, including passive zones, and is always present in:
- Modern `TrackerEntity` (but not modern `ScannerEntity`)

The `in_zones` state attribute is never present in:
- Modern `ScannerEntity`
- Legacy device trackers

Rationale:
Useful for triggers and conditions checking if a `device_tracker` or `person` entity is in some given zone

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds an `in_zones` state attribute for `device_tracker` entities so automations can evaluate membership in one or more zones (not just the closest zone), leveraging a new zone helper to compute all active zones for a coordinate.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `ATTR_IN_ZONES` and exposes it via `homeassistant.components.device_tracker`.
- Adds `zone.async_active_zones()` returning `(closest_zone, active_zone_entity_ids)` and uses it to populate `in_zones`.
- Updates config-entry device tracker tests to assert `in_zones` for `TrackerEntity` states.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/components/device_tracker/test_config_entry.py | Extends tracker entity expectations to include `ATTR_IN_ZONES` and validates values for sample zone setups. |
| homeassistant/components/zone/__init__.py | Adds `async_active_zones()` to return both closest zone and all active zones; keeps `async_active_zone()` as a wrapper. |
| homeassistant/components/device_tracker/legacy.py | Stores and exposes `_in_zones` for legacy GPS trackers and populates it from `async_active_zones()`. |
| homeassistant/components/device_tracker/const.py | Adds the new `ATTR_IN_ZONES` constant. |
| homeassistant/components/device_tracker/config_entry.py | Adds `ATTR_IN_ZONES` to modern `TrackerEntity` state attributes based on `async_active_zones()`. |
| homeassistant/components/device_tracker/__init__.py | Re-exports `ATTR_IN_ZONES`. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.


<details>

```

    (
        "battery_level",

        "expected_attributes",
    ),
    [

            None,

                ATTR_LONGITUDE: 2.0,
            },
        ),
        (
            None,
            None,
            50.0,
            60.0,
            STATE_HOME,
            {
                ATTR_SOURCE_TYPE: SourceType.GPS,
                ATTR_GPS_ACCURACY: 0,
                ATTR_IN_ZONES: ["zone.home"],
                ATTR_LATITUDE: 50.0,
                ATTR_LONGITUDE: 60.0,
            },
        ),
        (
            None,

                ATTR_SOURCE_TYPE: SourceType.GPS,
                ATTR_GPS_ACCURACY: 0,
                ATTR_IN_ZONES: ["zone.other_zone"],
                ATTR_LATITUDE: -50.0,
                ATTR_LONGITUDE: -60.0,
            },
        ),
```
**homeassistant/components/device_tracker/config_entry.py:306**
* Keep the `state_attributes` typing consistent with the base tracker entity while still allowing a list-valued attribute by using a more precise return type (e.g., include `list[str]` in the value union) rather

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 22 out of 22 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**homeassistant/components/zone/__init__.py:136**
* Sort `zone_entity_ids` (or include `entity_id` as a final sort key) before iterating so zone selection and `in_zones` ordering are deterministic when zones have equal distance/radius; `hass.states.async_entity_ids()` does not guarantee a stable order.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR modernizes the OurGroceries integration by migrating coordinator storage from `hass.data` to `ConfigEntry.runtime_data`, aligning it with current Home Assistant integration patterns.

**Changes:**
- Store the `OurGroceriesDataUpdateCoordinator` on `entry.runtime_data` during setup and rely on core unload behavior to clear it.
- Update the todo platform to fetch the coordinator from `entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data`.
- Adjust tests to import `DOMAIN`/exceptions from their canonical sources.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/ourgroceries/__init__.py | Store coordinator in `entry.runtime_data` and simplify unload to just unload platforms. |
| homeassistant/components/ourgroceries/todo.py | Switch coordinator retrieval to `entry.runtime_data` and type the entry accordingly. |
| tests/components/ourgroceries/conftest.py | Import `DOMAIN` from `const` since it’s no longer re-exported from the integration module. |
| tests/components/ourgroceries/test_init.py | Import exception types from their original libraries to match what the integration catches. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the `osoenergy` integration from storing the client in `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry_id]` to using `ConfigEntry.runtime_data` with a typed config entry alias.

**Changes:**
- Introduce `OSOEnergyConfigEntry = ConfigEntry[OSOEnergy]` and annotate `async_setup_entry`/`async_unload_entry` accordingly.
- Store the `OSOEnergy` client on `entry.runtime_data` during setup and read it from runtime_data in platform modules.
- Remove `hass.data` bookkeeping (`setdefault` / `pop`) and redundant `DOMAIN`/`ConfigEntry` imports in platforms.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/osoenergy/__init__.py` | Defines typed config entry alias, sets `entry.runtime_data`, and simplifies unload logic to only unload platforms. |
| `homeassistant/components/osoenergy/binary_sensor.py` | Switches platform setup to use `entry.runtime_data` and updates type annotation of the config entry parameter. |
| `homeassistant/components/osoenergy/sensor.py` | Switches platform setup to use `entry.runtime_data` and updates type annotation of the config entry parameter. |
| `homeassistant/components/osoenergy/water_heater.py` | Switches platform setup to use `entry.runtime_data` and updates type annotation of the config entry parameter. |

## Pull request overview

This PR refactors the OpenGarage integration to store and access the `OpenGarageDataUpdateCoordinator` via `ConfigEntry.runtime_data` (with a typed `OpenGarageConfigEntry` alias), instead of using `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry_id]`.

**Changes:**
- Add `OpenGarageConfigEntry = ConfigEntry[OpenGarageDataUpdateCoordinator]` and store the coordinator on `entry.runtime_data`.
- Update all OpenGarage platforms (binary_sensor/button/cover/sensor) to retrieve the coordinator from `entry.runtime_data`.
- Simplify unload logic by removing `hass.data` cleanup that is no longer needed.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/opengarage/__init__.py | Introduces the typed config entry alias and moves coordinator storage to `entry.runtime_data`. |
| homeassistant/components/opengarage/binary_sensor.py | Switches coordinator lookup from `hass.data` to `entry.runtime_data`. |
| homeassistant/components/opengarage/button.py | Switches coordinator lookup from `hass.data` to `entry.runtime_data`. |
| homeassistant/components/opengarage/cover.py | Switches coordinator lookup from `hass.data` to `entry.runtime_data`. |
| homeassistant/components/opengarage/sensor.py | Switches coordinator lookup from `hass.data` to `entry.runtime_data`. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the `ondilo_ico` integration to use `ConfigEntry.runtime_data` for storing and accessing the integration’s coordinator, aligning it with current Home Assistant integration patterns and avoiding `hass.data` usage.

**Changes:**
- Store the `OndiloIcoPoolsCoordinator` on `entry.runtime_data` during `async_setup_entry`.
- Update the sensor platform setup to read the coordinator from `entry.runtime_data` and type the entry accordingly.
- Avoid a potential circular import by importing `DOMAIN` from `.const` in the coordinator module.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/ondilo_ico/__init__.py` | Defines a typed config entry alias and stores the pools coordinator in `entry.runtime_data`; simplifies unload by removing `hass.data` cleanup. |
| `homeassistant/components/ondilo_ico/sensor.py` | Switches sensor setup to use the typed config entry and pulls the pools coordinator from `entry.runtime_data`. |
| `homeassistant/components/ondilo_ico/coordinator.py` | Imports `DOMAIN` from `.const` instead of from the package to prevent circular dependency issues. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

Use runtime_data in ondilo_ico integration

title: Add multi-plane support for Forecast.Solar integration
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

This PR implements multi-plane support for the Forecast.Solar integration using the **Config Subentry** architecture. This allows users to manage multiple solar panel arrays (planes) as independent subentries of a single configuration entry while maintaining a unified set of entities via an aggregating coordinator.

**Background:**
Many solar installations have panels facing different directions (e.g., east/west split, or panels on multiple roof sections). The Forecast.Solar API supports querying multiple planes in a single request for paid subscribers (Personal Plus tier and above), but the Home Assistant integration currently only supports a single plane configuration.

**What this PR does:**
- Adds plane management using Home Assistant's native subentry system, where each plane is managed as a subentry, allowing for independent addition, removal, and reconfiguration
- Allows configuring up to 4 planes total (1 main + 3 additional), matching Forecast.Solar's Professional Plus tier limits
- Implements multi-plane URL building to query all planes in a single API request
- Preserves plane configurations even if the API key is temporarily removed
- Shows a helpful message when users try to add planes without an API key

**User experience:**
1. Users without an API key see the menu but get an informative message when trying to add planes
2. Users with an API key can add additional planes through a simple form (declination, azimuth, power)
3. All configured planes are combined into a single API request for efficient forecasting

**Technical notes:**
- ~Includes a `MultiPlaneForecastSolar` class that extends the library's `ForecastSolar` to support multi-plane URLs~
- ~The code is prepared for easy migration when the upstream `forecast_solar` library adds native multi-plane support (PR home-assistant-libs/forecast_solar#275)~
- All changes are backward compatible - existing single-plane configurations continue to work unchanged

## Pull request overview

This pull request adds multi-plane support to the Forecast.Solar integration, allowing users with paid API subscriptions to configure multiple solar panel arrays with different orientations.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a menu-based options flow with three options: Settings, Add plane, and Remove plane
- Supports up to 4 planes total (1 main + 3 additional), matching Forecast.Solar API limits
- Upgrades forecast-solar library from 4.2.0 to 5.0.0 to support the Plane class
- Preserves plane configurations even when API key is temporarily removed

### Reviewed changes

| homeassistant/components/forecast_solar/coordinator.py | Builds Plane objects from config and passes them to ForecastSolar API client |
| homeassistant/components/forecast_solar/const.py | Adds CONF_PLANES constant |
| homeassistant/components/forecast_solar/strings.json | Adds user-facing strings for menu options, plane management, and abort reasons |
| homeassistant/components/forecast_solar/manifest.json | Updates forecast-solar library version to 5.0.0 |
| requirements_all.txt | Updates forecast-solar library version to 5.0.0 |
| requirements_test_all.txt | Updates forecast-solar library version to 5.0.0 |
| tests/components/forecast_solar/conftest.py | Fixes API key length in mock fixture to match regex requirement (16 chars) |
| tests/components/forecast_solar/test_config_flow.py | Adds comprehensive tests for plane management and updates existing tests for menu-based flow |
</details>

commits.message:
Add multi-plane support for Forecast.Solar integration

Enable configuring multiple solar planes when an API key is provided.
The options flow now shows a menu with Settings, Add plane, and Remove
plane options. Up to 4 planes total (1 main + 3 additional) are supported.

Changes:
- Add CONF_PLANES constant for storing additional plane configurations
- Extend options flow with menu, add_plane, and remove_plane steps
- Add Plane dataclass and MultiPlaneForecastSolar class to coordinator
- Update strings.json with new UI strings
- Add comprehensive tests for multi-plane functionality

Co-authored-by: Junie <noreply@jb.gg>

Bump forecast-solar to 5.0.0

Use native multi-plane support from the library, removing the
temporary Plane and MultiPlaneForecastSolar workarounds.

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Use deepcopy for planes in async_step_settings

Use deepcopy when preserving planes configuration in
async_step_settings for consistency with async_step_add_plane
and async_step_remove_plane, preventing unexpected behavior
from shared mutable references.

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Add subentry support for Forecast.Solar planes

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Use safe default for modules_power in v2→v3 migration

Fix test_subentry_flow_no_api_key to use version=3

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Use a constant instead of a string literal

Use entry directly

Remove unnecessary async block

Use NumberSelector and add_suggested_values_to_schema for plane fields

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Set default suggested value for Total Watt peak to 10000

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Extract default values for settings into constants

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Add validation for damping and inverter size in options flow

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Use entry directly in test_config_flow

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Simplify plane count check using len(entry.subentries)

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Rewrite migration tests to use explicit assertions instead of snapshots

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Use api_key_present fixture to reduce test duplication

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>
Use a constant instead of a string literal

Remove unnecessary async block

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Throw config error if multiple planes configured without API key

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Move config validation to coordinator as UpdateFailed

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Add missing reconfigure_successful translation for forecast_solar plane subentry

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Use async_update_and_abort for forecast_solar plane subentry reconfiguration

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Include subentries in forecast_solar diagnostics output

Add subentries data (declination, azimuth, modules_power, title) to the diagnostics config entry output and update the snapshot to match the test fixture.

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Enforce API key requirement for multiple solar planes

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Fix mypy union-attr error in Forecast.Solar coordinator

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Format Forecast.Solar coordinator code as suggested by hooks

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Move configuration validation before coordinator creation

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Explicitly reload forecast_solar when reconfiguring plane without update listeners

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Add test for multi-plane coordinator initialization

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Format Forecast.Solar config flow code

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Fix AsyncMock serialization in multi-plane initialization test

Co-authored-by: Junie <junie@jetbrains.com>

Add tests for 100% coverage of forecast_solar integration

Add mock_forecast_solar fixture to migration tests in forecast_solar

Apply get_subentries_of_type in forecast_solar

Format forecast_solar with ruff

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes how the `person` entity determines `gps_accuracy` so it matches the chosen coordinate source (e.g., home zone coordinates when “home” comes from a stationary tracker), addressing a regression from #134075.

**Changes:**
- Use the coordinates source (zone/tracker) rather than the state source (tracker) to populate `gps_accuracy`.
- Extend the person component tests to ensure `gps_accuracy` is not incorrectly inherited from a non-GPS tracker when coordinates come from the home zone.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/person/__init__.py` | Fixes `gps_accuracy` propagation by reading it from the coordinate-providing state. |
| `tests/components/person/test_init.py` | Adds coverage for the “home zone coordinates + no gps_accuracy” behavior and guards against regression. |

commits.message: Fix propagation of GPS accuracy in person entity

title: Migrate openhome to use runtime_data
body:
## Proposed change

Migrate the openhome integration to use `config_entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data[DOMAIN]` for storing the `Device` instance.

- Add `OpenhomeConfigEntry` type alias (`ConfigEntry[Device]`)
- Store device in `config_entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry_id]`
- Update `media_player.py` and `update.py` to read device from `config_entry.runtime_data`
- Simplify `async_unload_entry` by removing manual `hass.data` cleanup
- Remove unused `ConfigEntry` imports from platform files

## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the Openhome integration to store its `Device` instance on `ConfigEntry.runtime_data`, aligning it with current Home Assistant integration patterns and reducing reliance on `hass.data`.

**Changes:**
- Introduce `OpenhomeConfigEntry = ConfigEntry[Device]` and store the device on `config_entry.runtime_data`.
- Update platform setup (`media_player.py`, `update.py`) to read the device from `config_entry.runtime_data`.
- Simplify `async_unload_entry` by removing manual `hass.data` cleanup.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/openhome/__init__.py` | Defines the typed config entry alias, stores the device in `runtime_data`, and simplifies unload. |
| `homeassistant/components/openhome/media_player.py` | Switches platform setup to use `config_entry.runtime_data`. |
| `homeassistant/components/openhome/update.py` | Switches platform setup to use `config_entry.runtime_data` (and retains update entity logic). |

commits.message:
Migrate openhome to use runtime_data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the Picnic integration from `hass.data[DOMAIN]` storage to `ConfigEntry.runtime_data`, aligning it with the newer Home Assistant integration pattern for per-entry runtime objects.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `PicnicRuntimeData` and `PicnicConfigEntry` and stores the API client + coordinator on `entry.runtime_data`.
- Updates platforms (sensor/todo) and tests to access the coordinator via `config_entry.runtime_data`.
- Removes unused `CONF_API` / `CONF_COORDINATOR` constants.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/picnic/coordinator.py` | Adds `PicnicRuntimeData` + `PicnicConfigEntry` typing for runtime data storage. |
| `homeassistant/components/picnic/__init__.py` | Writes runtime objects to `entry.runtime_data` and removes `hass.data` usage/unload cleanup. |
| `homeassistant/components/picnic/sensor.py` | Switches sensor setup to read coordinator from `config_entry.runtime_data`. |
| `homeassistant/components/picnic/todo.py` | Switches todo setup to read coordinator from `config_entry.runtime_data`. |
| `homeassistant/components/picnic/services.py` | Switches service lookup to fetch the entry and return `entry.runtime_data.api_client`. |
| `homeassistant/components/picnic/const.py` | Removes unused constants previously used for `hass.data` storage keys. |
| `tests/components/picnic/test_sensor.py` | Updates tests to reference coordinator via `config_entry.runtime_data`. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

commits.message:
Migrate picnic to use runtime_data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Drop PicnicRuntimeData, use coordinator as runtime_data directly

The coordinator already stores the API client as picnic_api_client,
making the PicnicRuntimeData wrapper redundant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the `nibe_heatpump` integration to use `ConfigEntry.runtime_data` for storing and accessing the `CoilCoordinator`, replacing the previous `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry_id]` pattern.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a `NibeHeatpumpConfigEntry` type alias (`ConfigEntry[CoilCoordinator]`) to type `runtime_data` access consistently.
- Stores the coordinator in `entry.runtime_data` during setup and updates all platforms to read it from `config_entry.runtime_data`.
- Simplifies unload by removing manual `hass.data` cleanup and removes now-unneeded imports in platforms.

- Add `OpenexchangeratesConfigEntry` type alias in `coordinator.py`
- Store coordinator in `entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data[DOMAIN]`
- Replace `hass.data.get(DOMAIN, {})` lookup with `hass.config_entries.async_loaded_entries(DOMAIN)` to find existing coordinators by API key
- Read coordinator from `config_entry.runtime_data` in `sensor.py`
- Simplify `async_unload_entry` by removing manual `hass.data` cleanup
- Remove unused `ConfigEntry` import from `sensor.py`

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the `openexchangerates` integration from storing its coordinator in `hass.data[DOMAIN]` to using `ConfigEntry.runtime_data`, aligning it with current Home Assistant integration patterns.

**Changes:**
- Introduces an `OpenexchangeratesConfigEntry` type alias and threads it through setup/coordinator/sensor typing.
- Stores the coordinator on `entry.runtime_data` and updates the sensor platform to read it from there.
- Reworks “existing coordinators for this API key” discovery to use `hass.config_entries.async_loaded_entries(DOMAIN)` and simplifies unload by removing manual `hass.data` cleanup.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/openexchangerates/__init__.py | Switches coordinator storage to `entry.runtime_data`, updates coordinator discovery, and simplifies unload. |
| homeassistant/components/openexchangerates/coordinator.py | Adds `OpenexchangeratesConfigEntry` alias and updates coordinator typing to match runtime_data usage. |
| homeassistant/components/openexchangerates/sensor.py | Reads coordinator from `config_entry.runtime_data` and updates typing/imports accordingly. |

commits.message:
Migrate openexchangerates to use runtime_data

## Pull request overview

Adds a `shell_command.reload` service to let the `shell_command` integration refresh its YAML-defined commands at runtime, aligning it with other YAML-configured integrations that support reload.

**Changes:**
- Refactors service registration to use a per-command handler factory (`_make_handler`).
- Registers an admin-only `reload` service that re-reads YAML config and re-registers shell_command services.
- Adds service metadata (`strings.json`, new `icons.json`) and a test covering reload behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/shell_command/__init__.py` | Adds reload service + refactors command handlers to be generated per service registration. |
| `homeassistant/components/shell_command/strings.json` | Documents the new `reload` service for the UI/service docs. |
| `homeassistant/components/shell_command/icons.json` | Adds an icon mapping for the new `reload` service. |
| `tests/components/shell_command/test_init.py` | Adds a test asserting reload swaps out services based on updated YAML. |

## Pull request overview

This PR updates a set of core entity-domain `strings.json` files to remove redundant field descriptions from device automation triggers/conditions, relying on field names (and frontend-provided context) instead.

**Changes:**
- Remove `description` entries for common trigger/condition fields (notably `behavior` and `threshold`) across many domains.
- Adjust common field *names* to be more self-explanatory without descriptions (e.g., “Test true when”, “Trigger when”, “Threshold type”).
- Keep existing selector options and automation descriptions intact while simplifying field metadata.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 38 out of 38 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |

| homeassistant/components/alarm_control_panel/strings.json | Remove redundant trigger/condition field descriptions; update common field labels. |
| homeassistant/components/assist_satellite/strings.json | Remove redundant trigger/condition field descriptions; update common field labels. |
| homeassistant/components/battery/strings.json | Remove redundant behavior/threshold field descriptions; update common field labels. |
| homeassistant/components/calendar/strings.json | Remove redundant condition field description; update common field label. |
| homeassistant/components/climate/strings.json | Remove redundant behavior/threshold field descriptions; update common field labels. |
| homeassistant/components/counter/strings.json | Remove redundant trigger/condition field descriptions; update common trigger label. |
| homeassistant/components/cover/strings.json | Remove redundant trigger/condition field descriptions; update common field labels. |
| homeassistant/components/device_tracker/strings.json | Rem

There are some missing that were pointed by copilot.

Also "Test true when" sounds grammatically weird. Maybe "Pass when", "True when" or "Condition passes when"?

Copilot reviewed 38 out of 38 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR hardens the Backup upload flow by sanitizing multipart upload filenames so received backups are written only inside Home Assistant’s temporary backup directory, preventing path traversal.

**Changes:**
- Sanitize `suggested_filename` when receiving an uploaded backup by stripping directory components.
- Add a new parametrized test covering common `../` and nested-path traversal attempts.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/backup/manager.py` | Sanitizes the uploaded multipart filename before constructing the temp backup path. |
| `tests/components/backup/test_manager.py` | Adds coverage to ensure traversal attempts via directory components are sanitized. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
If we add some more test cases as suggested above, I think this is good to go.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Thanks!

commits.message:
Store received backup in temp backup dir only

Sanitize the suggested filename from multipart upload requests by
stripping directory components, preventing path traversal that could
write files outside the temp backup directory.
Verify open files to match expected paths exactly

Use unquoted FormData to test raw path traversal filenames

Use FormData(quote_fields=False) to send raw traversal filenames
to the server, ensuring the server-side sanitization is actually
exercised.

Add guard for .. filename

Add guard for .. in filenames. Technically it would create a file named
like this and not traverse directories. But it doesn't seem useful so
block it as well. Also check . and .. in tests to validate they don't
cause issues.
Use PureWindowsPath to sanetize Windows paths too

## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the `pvoutput` integration from storing its `PVOutputDataUpdateCoordinator` in `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry.entry_id]` to using `ConfigEntry.runtime_data`, aligning it with current Home Assistant integration patterns.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a typed config entry alias (`PvOutputConfigEntry`) for the integration’s coordinator.
- Stores the coordinator on `entry.runtime_data` during setup and removes `hass.data` usage.
- Updates sensor and diagnostics code paths to read the coordinator from `entry.runtime_data`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/pvoutput/__init__.py | Stores coordinator on `entry.runtime_data` and simplifies unload (no `hass.data` cleanup). |
| homeassistant/components/pvoutput/coordinator.py | Adds `PvOutputConfigEntry` type alias and types the coordinator’s config entry usage. |
| homeassistant/components/pvoutput/sensor.py | Reads coordinator from `entry.runtime_data` and updates entry typing. |
| homeassistant/components/pvoutput/diagnostics.py | Reads coordinator data from `entry.runtime_data` and updates entry typing/imports. |

commits.message:
Migrate pvoutput to use runtime_data

This PR updates the Obihai integration to use `ConfigEntry.runtime_data` to store and retrieve the `ObihaiConnection` instance instead of using `hass.data`.

**Changes:**
- Introduce a typed `ObihaiConfigEntry = ConfigEntry[ObihaiConnection]` alias for stronger typing around `runtime_data`.
- Store the `ObihaiConnection` on `entry.runtime_data` during `async_setup_entry`.
- Update sensor and button platforms to read the connection from `entry.runtime_data` (removing `hass.data[DOMAIN][entry_id]` usage).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |

| `homeassistant/components/obihai/sensor.py` | Fetch the `ObihaiConnection` from `entry.runtime_data` during platform setup. |
| `homeassistant/components/obihai/button.py` | Fetch the `ObihaiConnection` from `entry.runtime_data` during platform setup. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
Thanks @epenet 👍

- Updated test file docstring in `tests/components/temper/test_sensor.py` to use "TEMPer integration" terminology

title: Use Tesla Fleet API for Tessie config flow validation
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

Switch Tessie config-flow token validation from `tessie_api.get_state_of_all_vehicles` to `tesla_fleet_api.tessie.Tessie.list_vehicles`.
This keeps the user and reauth flow behavior unchanged while mapping `InvalidToken` and `MissingToken` to the existing invalid token error and preserving the current network and unknown error handling.
The config-flow tests now patch the Tesla Fleet API path directly and cover the new token exception mapping.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the Tessie config-flow token validation from the deprecated `tessie_api.get_state_of_all_vehicles` function to the `tesla_fleet_api.tessie.Tessie.list_vehicles` method. The changes maintain the same user-facing behavior while updating the underlying API calls and exception mapping.

**Changes:**
- Replaced `tessie_api.get_state_of_all_vehicles` with `tesla_fleet_api.tessie.Tessie.list_vehicles` for token validation
- Created a new `_async_validate_access_token` helper function to centralize validation logic
- Updated exception mapping: `InvalidToken` and `MissingToken` exceptions are now mapped to the same error as the previous auth error
- Refactored config flow steps to use the new validation function
- Updated tests to mock the Tesla Fleet API path and use the new exception classes

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/tessie/config_flow.py | Migrated validation from tessie_api to tesla_fleet_api, created helper function for token validation |
| tests/components/tessie/test_config_flow.py | Updated fixtures and tests to mock Tessie.list_vehicles and use new exception types |

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `172ef0b746`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a grammar error in the `victron_ble` integration's error message. The original message used incorrect verb form "to shutdown" (should be "to shut down"), but rather than using two separate words, the PR restructures the sentence to use "shutdown" as a noun, making it consistent with related error messages in the same file.

**Changes:**
- Updated the `input_shutdown_failure` error message from "PV input failed to shutdown" to "PV input shutdown failed" for grammatical consistency

## Pull request overview

Re-introduces the `bmw_connected_drive` integration as a minimal “skeleton” integration that only raises a Repairs issue, guiding users to remove existing config entries since BMW access is no longer possible.

**Changes:**
- Added a new `bmw_connected_drive` integration package that creates a non-fixable Repairs issue on config entry setup.
- Added user-facing issue text via `strings.json` describing the removal and linking to an alternative custom component.
- Updated hassfest quality scale allowlists to account for the (now-internal/legacy) skeleton integration.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `script/hassfest/quality_scale.py` | Adds `bmw_connected_drive` to the relevant hassfest allowlists for integrations without quality scale definitions/scale grading. |
| `homeassistant/components/bmw_connected_drive/__init__.py` | Implements the skeleton integration behavior: create/delete a Repairs issue tied to config entries. |
| `homeassistant/components/bmw_connected_drive/config_flow.py` | Adds a minimal config flow class (no interactive steps) consistent with other “removed integration” skeletons. |
| `homeassistant/components/bmw_connected_drive/manifest.json` | Adds a minimal manifest marking the integration as `system` / `legacy`. |
| `homeassistant/components/bmw_connected_drive/strings.json` | Adds the Repairs issue title/description with placeholders for entry management and an alternative component link. |
</details>
Thanks! Please add the same tests as for the myq integration. Then good to go.

## Pull request overview

This pull request updates the `solarlog_cli` library dependency from version 0.7.0 to 0.7.1 to fix authentication errors that occur with Solar-Log devices running specific firmware versions. The fix addresses issue #160991 where users experienced connection failures after upgrading to Home Assistant 2026.1.1.

**Changes:**
- Update solarlog_cli dependency from 0.7.0 to 0.7.1 across all dependency configuration files
- Resolves firmware-specific authentication errors in the Solar-Log integration

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/solarlog/manifest.json | Updated solarlog_cli requirement to 0.7.1 |
| requirements_all.txt | Updated solarlog_cli to 0.7.1 |
| requirements_test_all.txt | Updated solarlog_cli to 0.7.1 |
Thanks @dontinelli 👍

## Pull request overview

Adds a new brand entry for **BEGA** to Home Assistant’s brand metadata, enabling consistent brand display/lookup and IoT standard tagging.

**Changes:**
- Added `homeassistant/brands/bega.json` defining the BEGA brand (domain/name + Zigbee standard).
- Updated the generated `homeassistant/generated/integrations.json` to include the new `bega` entry.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 1 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/generated/integrations.json` | Registers the BEGA brand entry in the generated integrations/brands index. |
| `homeassistant/brands/bega.json` | Adds the BEGA brand definition with `iot_standards: ["zigbee"]`. |

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR removes the Dropbox integration from Home Assistant Core to prevent it from being shipped in 2026.4 while unresolved account-linking issues are addressed.

**Changes:**
- Delete the Dropbox integration implementation (OAuth/application_credentials support + backup agent) and its translations/quality metadata.
- Remove Dropbox integration tests and eliminate the `python-dropbox-api` dependency from the requirements lockfiles.
- Update generated registries/lists to no longer include the `dropbox` domain (integrations, config flows, application credentials) and drop strict-typing/codeowner entries.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 19 out of 22 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `tests/components/dropbox/test_init.py` | Removes Dropbox integration setup/unload tests. |
| `tests/components/dropbox/test_config_flow.py` | Removes Dropbox OAuth config flow/reauth tests. |
| `tests/components/dropbox/test_backup.py` | Removes Dropbox backup agent behavior tests. |
| `tests/components/dropbox/conftest.py` | Removes shared Dropbox test fixtures (credentials + client mocks). |
| `tests/components/dropbox/__init__.py` | Removes Dropbox test package marker. |
| `requirements_test_all.txt` | Drops `python-dropbox-api` from test requirements. |
| `requirements_all.txt` | Drops `python-dropbox-api` from full requirements. |
| `mypy.ini` | Removes mypy strict settings section for `homeassistant.components.dropbox.*`. |
| `homeassistant/generated/integrations.json` | Removes the `dropbox` integration entry from generated integrations registry. |
| `homeassistant/generated/config_flows.py` | Removes `dropbox` from generated config flow domain list. |
| `homeassistant/generated/application_credentials.py` | Removes `dropbox` from generated application_credentials domains list. |
| `homeassistant/components

title: Bump ZHA to 1.1.1
body:
## Proposed change

This bumps ZHA to [1.1.1](https://github.com/zigpy/zha/releases/tag/1.1.1) (full diff: https://github.com/zigpy/zha/compare/1.1.0...1.1.1).
These dependency upgrades are bundled with it: 

- `zigpy` [1.2.2](https://github.com/zigpy/zigpy/releases/tag/1.2.2)
full diff: https://github.com/zigpy/zigpy/compare/1.2.1...1.2.2

- `zha-quirks` [1.1.1](https://github.com/zigpy/zha-device-handlers/releases/tag/1.1.1)
full diff: https://github.com/zigpy/zha-device-handlers/compare/1.1.0...1.1.1

**This should go into the 2026.4.0 beta/release.** All changes were verified with physical devices.
This is a patch release, so **no** new (quirks v2) entities are added in this release.

A quick overview of the changes in this ZHA release:
- Fixed an issue where the `setpoint_change_source_timestamp` attribute on the `Thermostat` cluster was not initialized, leading to an "Unknown" timestamp entity on some TRVs
- Fixed long debug log messages for emitted OTA events
- Fixed Ubisys H1 TRV using the wrong datatype for the `season` attribute
- Fixed Third Reality curtain gen2 having a wrong `max_value`
- Improved the friendly name for Heiman smoke sensors (that use a different one on old firmwares)
- Improved support for a Sonoff remote variant

### Possible conflict with Serialx bump (outdated; see EDIT)

Depending on when this is merged, it may conflict with the Serialx bump (https://github.com/home-assistant/core/pull/167023).
Unlike the ZHA bump, the Serialx bump does NOT need to go into the beta/release.

However, I did verify that the new Serialx version also works fine. It doesn't really touch anything ZHA and the Connect hardware integrations use, so if it's easiest to also cherry-pick that, it *should* be fine to include as well.
Otherwise, I could also create another PR targeting `rc` with just this ZHA bump.

EDIT: I merged this PR first, so it can be cleanly cherry-picked into beta/`rc`. The conflict in the Serialx PR bump is fixed as well now (https://github.com/home-assistant/core/pull/167023).

title: Migrate google_mail OAuth token refresh exception handling
body:
Fixes #163460

## Breaking change
None

## Proposed change
Migrate `google_mail` token refresh error handling to the OAuth helper exception model introduced in 2026.3.

Technical changes:
- Handle `OAuth2TokenRequestReauthError` explicitly:
  - During setup (`SETUP_IN_PROGRESS`): raise `ConfigEntryAuthFailed`
  - During runtime: start reauth flow and raise `HomeAssistantError`
- Handle `OAuth2TokenRequestError` explicitly:
  - During setup: raise `ConfigEntryNotReady`
  - During runtime: raise `HomeAssistantError`
- Preserve legacy fallback behavior for `RefreshError`
- Treat legacy `ClientError` / `ClientResponseError` as `non-reauth` errors

Tests:
- Add focused unit tests for `AsyncConfigEntryAuth.check_and_refresh_token()` covering setup/runtime behavior for new OAuth exceptions and legacy fallbacks.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the Google Mail integration’s OAuth token refresh handling to align with the newer OAuth helper exception model (2026.3+), ensuring correct setup vs runtime behavior and adding unit tests to lock in the mappings.

**Changes:**
- Update `AsyncConfigEntryAuth.check_and_refresh_token()` to explicitly handle `OAuth2TokenRequestReauthError` (setup: `ConfigEntryAuthFailed`, runtime: start reauth + `HomeAssistantError`) and `OAuth2TokenRequestError` (setup: `ConfigEntryNotReady`, runtime: `HomeAssistantError`).
- Preserve legacy handling for `RefreshError` / `ClientError` / `ClientResponseError`, including expanding runtime reauth triggering from only HTTP 400 to any 4xx.
- Add unit tests covering success, new OAuth exceptions (setup/runtime), and legacy reauth-triggering cases.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/google_mail/api.py` | Migrates token refresh exception handling to the new OAuth helper exception types while retaining legacy fallbacks. |
| `tests/components/google_mail/test_api.py` | Adds focused unit tests for `check_and_refresh_token()` across setup/runtime for new and legacy exception paths. |





---

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).
This looks good to me, thanks @Oluwatobi-Mustapha!

Instead we should test this via patching the methods and setting up the integration like normal, instead of testing just the `api` file. As we want to test the behavior of the whole integration and not just of the api file

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**homeassistant/components/google_mail/api.py:73**
* Catch `google.auth.exceptions.RefreshError` again in `check_and_refresh_token`, since it no longer matches the `except ClientError` branch and will currently bypass the legacy reauth/NotReady handling (the `isinstance(ex, RefreshError)` check is unreachable).
```
        except ClientError as ex:
            if setup_in_progress:
                raise ConfigEntryNotReady from ex
            status = getattr(ex, "status", None)
            if isinstance(ex, RefreshError) or (
                isinstance(status, int)
                and 400 <= status < 500
                and status != HTTPStatus.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
            ):
                self.oauth_session.config_entry.async_start_reauth(
                    self.oauth_session.hass
                )
            raise HomeAssistantError(ex) from ex
        return self.access_token
```
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**homeassistant/components/google_mail/api.py:66**
* Trigger reauth (and avoid setup retries) for legacy `ClientResponseError` auth failures. With the current logic, a `ClientResponseError` (e.g. 401) falls into the `(RefreshError, ClientError)` block and never calls `async_start_reauth`, so runtime updates won't start reauth and setup will incorrectly raise `ConfigEntryNotReady` instead of `ConfigEntryAuthFailed` for 4xx auth errors.
```
        except (RefreshError, ClientError) as ex:
            if setup_in_progress:
                raise ConfigEntryNotReady from ex
            if isinstance(ex, RefreshError):
                self.oauth_session.config_entry.async_start_reauth(
                    self.oauth_session.hass
                )
            raise HomeAssistantError(ex) from ex
```
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
Looks good! Thanks!

tests: align google_mail reauth expectations with ClientError handling
google_mail: format reauth service test context manager

title: Wrap hassio import in is_hassio check in get_system_info helper
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->

## Proposed change

The `async_get_system_info` helper always loads the `hassio` component without consideration for whether Supervisor is in use in this system or not. This can create a secret dependency on the component and the `aiohasupervisor` library it uses. Most notably, bootstrap calls this helper in order to get various environment and system info cached:
https://github.com/home-assistant/core/blob/f74256db2da4a16486f1ad1331ebe4779ebc86f5/homeassistant/bootstrap.py#L486

This breaks on installations without Supervisor as shown in #166976 .

Normally we use the `is_hassio` helper  that checks if the `hassio` component has been loaded combined with a local import to solve this. This seems problematic because this early in bootstrap the `hassio` component has not been loaded, nothing has. However `hassio` is only needed to enrich the response of this helper and bootstrap tosses the entire response without caching it. So its still safe to use this pattern here, we aren't losing anything needed during bootstrap.

There's a short discussion [here](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues/166976#issuecomment-4172668734) and [in discord](https://discord.com/channels/330944238910963714/1488952602380075179/1488952650258190539) on some possible better alternative solutions that don't cause `aiohasupervisor` to be installed almost always even when there's no Supervisor. However this PR is takes the simplest fix so it can be safely included in current release, improved fixes can be tackled separately later if we wish.

## Pull request overview

Adjusts the `async_get_system_info` helper to avoid importing the Supervisor (`hassio`) integration during early bootstrap on non-Supervisor installs, preventing a hard dependency on `aiohasupervisor` and related import-time failures.

**Changes:**
- Moves the `hassio` import behind an `is_hassio(hass)` gate so it’s only imported when the integration is actually loaded.
- Removes the previous unconditional async import path that could pull in `hassio` during bootstrap.
OK once the CI is fixed.

Thanks @mdegat01 👍 

CI failures unrelated (telegram tests)

## Pull request overview

This pull request adds a reconfiguration flow to the Fresh-r integration, allowing users to update their account password without recreating the config entry. It also implements dynamic device handling, enabling the integration to properly track devices that are added or removed from the user's Fresh-r account. The changes include support for removing stale devices from the device registry and re-creating entities when previously removed devices reappear.

**Changes:**
- Added `async_step_reconfigure` method to handle password updates via reconfiguration flow
- Changed coordinator data structure from list to dictionary for efficient device tracking
- Implemented dynamic device detection that creates entities for new devices and cleans up for removed ones
- Added logic to automatically remove stale devices from the Home Assistant device registry
- Updated quality scale rules to reflect completed features (dynamic-devices, reconfiguration-flow, stale-devices)

### Reviewed changes

| homeassistant/components/freshr/sensor.py | Refactored to dynamically add/remove sensor entities as devices change |
| homeassistant/components/freshr/coordinator.py | Changed data type to dict and added stale device removal logic |

Only the password can be updated, due to the username being the uniqueid, so in essence it's the same as the reauth flow.

- Implement async_step_reconfigure to handle user password updates.
- Update quality scale to mark reconfiguration flow as complete.
- Add localization strings for reconfiguration success and form description.
- Enhance tests for successful reconfiguration and error handling.
Update scale to platinum now diagnostics is in, reconfigure is the last step

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the `serialx` dependency version used by the ZHA and Home Assistant Hardware integrations, and refreshes the generated requirements lockfiles accordingly.

**Changes:**
- Bump `serialx` from `0.6.2` to `1.1.1` in `homeassistant_hardware` and `zha` integration manifests.
- Update `requirements_all.txt` and `requirements_test_all.txt` to reflect the new pinned `serialx` version.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| requirements_test_all.txt | Updates the pinned `serialx` version used in the test requirements aggregate. |
| requirements_all.txt | Updates the pinned `serialx` version used in the full requirements aggregate. |
| homeassistant/components/zha/manifest.json | Bumps `serialx` requirement for the ZHA integration to `1.1.1`. |
| homeassistant/components/homeassistant_hardware/manifest.json | Bumps `serialx` requirement for the Home Assistant Hardware integration to `1.1.1`. |
Gave this a quick test run with ZBT-2 and a (non-ESPHome) network coordinator. Also flashed Zigbee + Thread firmware on the ZBT-2. Everything still works as expected.

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the module-level docstring in the Start.ca integration's `__init__.py` file to improve terminology and branding consistency. It changes the documentation comment from "The startca component" to "The Start.ca integration," reflecting both the terminology shift in Home Assistant (from "components" to "integrations") and the proper branding of the service.

**Changes:**
- Updated module docstring in `__init__.py` to use "integration" terminology and proper branding

| tests/components/denonavr/__init__.py | Updated module docstring to match the integration name used in production code |

## Pull request overview

This PR improves documentation and naming consistency in the DNS IP integration by renaming references from "component" to "integration" terminology and standardizing the use of "DNS IP" instead of "dnsip" in user-facing docstrings. These are documentation-only changes that do not affect code functionality.

**Changes:**
- Update module docstring in the main init.py from "The dnsip component" to "The DNS IP integration"
- Update the async_unload_entry docstring to use "DNS IP" instead of "dnsip"
- Standardize test module docstrings to use "DNS IP integration" terminology

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/dnsip/__init__.py | Updates module docstring and function docstring for consistency and terminology |
| tests/components/dnsip/test_init.py | Updates module docstring to use "integration" and "DNS IP" terminology |
| tests/components/dnsip/test_config_flow.py | Updates module docstring to improve naming from "dnsip" to "DNS IP" |
| tests/components/dnsip/__init__.py | Updates module docstring to use proper capitalization "DNS IP" |

**Changes:**
- Bump `srpenergy` dependency from `1.3.6` to `1.3.8` in the integration manifest.
- Update the generated dependency lock lists (`requirements_all.txt`, `requirements_test_all.txt`) to match the new pinned version.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/srp_energy/manifest.json` | Pins the `srpenergy` library to `1.3.8` for the integration. |
| `requirements_all.txt` | Updates the global generated requirements list to `srpenergy==1.3.8`. |
| `requirements_test_all.txt` | Updates the test generated requirements list to `srpenergy==1.3.8`. |
Thanks @ammmze 👍

fix: update srpenergy dependency to 1.3.8

This should resolve errors while fetching data from SRP. See lamoreauxlab/srpenergy-api-client-python#21.

## Pull request overview

This pull request improves string handling in the HTML5 integration by adding translation placeholders for action names that should not be translated and adding references for the tag field. The changes apply a consistent translation pattern across multiple related string definitions.

**Changes:**
- Add translation references for the "tag" field name in both the entity state attributes and dismiss_message service to reuse the definition from send_message service
- Update the deprecated_notify_action issue description to use placeholders for the new action names instead of hardcoded strings, preventing unwanted translation of action identifiers
- Add translation placeholders in the issue.py file to map the new_action_1 and new_action_2 placeholders to their actual action names

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/html5/strings.json | Updates tag field references and replaces hardcoded action names with placeholders in deprecated_notify_action description |
| homeassistant/components/html5/issue.py | Adds translation placeholders for new_action_1 and new_action_2 in deprecated_notify_action_call function |
Thanks @tr4nt0r 👍

title: Add command compatibility scaffold for Tessie migration
body:
## Proposed change
This PR adds a compatibility scaffold for the Tessie integration so legacy `tessie_api` command calls and new awaitable command style can coexist during migration. It centralizes command handling in `helpers.py`, updates the vehicle entity `run` path to support both call styles, and adds an optional `Vehicle` API handle on `TessieVehicleData`. A targeted init test was added to verify the optional API handle defaults to `None` in this scaffold stage.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds a migration scaffold in the Tessie integration to support both legacy `tessie_api` command calls and newer awaitable Fleet API command calls during a transition period.

**Changes:**
- Centralizes command exception handling and legacy command-result handling in `helpers.py`.
- Updates `TessieEntity.run()` to accept either a legacy command callable or an awaitable Fleet API command.
- Extends `TessieVehicleData` with an optional `api` handle and adds a test to confirm it defaults to `None`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/tessie/helpers.py` | Adds shared handlers for awaitable Fleet API commands and legacy `tessie_api` command responses. |
| `homeassistant/components/tessie/entity.py` | Updates `run()` to route either awaitable commands or legacy callables through the new helpers. |
| `homeassistant/components/tessie/models.py` | Adds an optional `Vehicle` API handle to `TessieVehicleData`. |
| `tests/components/tessie/test_init.py` | Adds a test to assert the new optional vehicle API handle defaults to `None`. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

commits.message: Tighten Tessie API handle scaffold test

## Pull request overview

This pull request updates mypy from version 1.19.1 to 1.20.0 and makes corresponding code changes to address mypy 1.20.0's stricter type checking. The PR also updates the librt dependency (an internal mypy dependency) from 0.7.3 to 0.8.1.

**Changes:**
- Updated mypy and librt versions in requirements_test.txt
- Corrected the `discard()` method signature in _ComponentSet to match the parent set class
- Added explicit type annotations to variables in several config flows and utilities to improve type checking
- Added `cast()` function calls for type narrowing in alarmdecoder config flow
- Added `# type: ignore` comments to suppress mypy 1.20.0 warnings in sonarr, lidarr, homekit, and other components

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 13 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| ---- | ----------- |

| homeassistant/components/sonarr/sensor.py | Added type: ignore[misc] comments for f-string type issues |
| homeassistant/components/sonarr/helpers.py | Added type: ignore[misc] comments for type narrowing |
| homeassistant/components/onkyo/coordinator.py | Removed unnecessary type: ignore[misc] comment |
