title: Add Off mode support for water_heater entities in HomeKit
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->

## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
Water heater entities exposed to HomeKit currently only support Heat as a target mode. This adds Off (value 0) from Apple's HAP specification for [`TargetHeatingCoolingState`](https://github.com/apple/HomeKitADK/blob/master/HAP/HAPCharacteristicTypes.h#L687-L722), which allows water heater devices to be turned off and on through the Home app, correctly reflects the on/off state back to HomeKit when changed in HA, and also provides parity with [Thermostat](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/blob/dev/homeassistant/components/homekit/type_thermostats.py#L190).

Changes:

- Adds `Off` (0) to `HC_HOMEKIT_VALID_MODES_WATER_HEATER` ( added conditionally via a per-entity valid_modes dict )
- Updates `set_heat_cool` to call `water_heater.turn_off` / `turn_on`
- Updates `async_update_state` to report the actual state to HomeKit

It seems you haven't yet signed a CLA. Please do so [here](https://home-assistant.io/developers/cla_sign_start/?pr=home-assistant/core#166836).

Once you do that we will be able to review and accept this pull request.

Thanks!

## Pull request overview

Adds HomeKit “Off” (0) support for water_heater entities by expanding valid TargetHeatingCoolingState values and wiring HomeKit mode changes into Home Assistant service calls, with tests covering the new behavior.

**Changes:**
- Add `Off` (0) to the valid HomeKit modes for water heaters.
- Update the HomeKit setter to call water heater `turn_off` / `turn_on` services when HomeKit mode changes.
- Update state reporting so HomeKit reflects “Off” vs “Heat” based on the HA state, plus add a dedicated test for Off mode.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/homekit/type_thermostats.py` | Adds Off to water-heater valid modes; updates setter/state sync to handle Off/Heat and call HA services. |
| `tests/components/homekit/test_type_thermostats.py` | Extends existing water heater test to expect `turn_on`, and adds a new test verifying Off mode behavior and state reflection. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**homeassistant/components/homekit/type_thermostats.py:889**
* Guard setting the HomeKit characteristics to Off so you don't attempt to set an invalid value when Off wasn't exposed.

`async_update_state` currently sets `HC_HEAT_COOL_OFF` whenever HA state is `STATE_OFF`, but `valid_values` only includes Off when `off_mode_available` is true. There are water_heater entities that can report state `off` without supporting ON_OFF/OPERATION_MODE (e.g., atag water heater sets `current_operation` to `STATE_OFF` while only advertising TARGET_TEMPERATURE), which would make `set_value(0)` raise `ValueError` here.
```
                self.char_current_heat_cool.set_value(HC_HEAT_COOL_HEAT)

    state: State, unit: str, default_min: float, default_max: float
) -> tuple[float, float]:
    """Calculate the temperature range from a state."""
```
</details>

commits.message:
Add Off mode support for water_heater entities in HomeKit

The HomeKit Thermostat service defines Off (value 0) as a valid target
heating/cooling state per Apple's HAP specification. The WaterHeater
class hardcoded valid modes to {"Heat": 1} only, preventing users from
turning off water heater devices via HomeKit.

Off is now dynamically exposed based on entity supported features:
- ON_OFF feature: uses water_heater.turn_off / turn_on
- OPERATION_MODE feature with "off" in operation list: uses
  set_operation_mode
- Unsupported mode values fall back to Heat

Changes:
- Dynamically add Off to valid modes when entity supports it
- Dispatch set_heat_cool based on ON_OFF vs OPERATION_MODE features
- Revert to Heat when Off is requested but not supported
- Update async_update_state to reflect Off state from HA to HomeKit
- Reload accessory when ATTR_OPERATION_LIST changes
- Add tests for ON_OFF path, OPERATION_MODE path, and no-off fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: growatt_server: implement reconfiguration flow (Gold)
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->

## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

Implements the `reconfiguration-flow` Gold quality scale rule for the `growatt_server` integration.

Adds a reconfiguration flow (`async_step_reconfigure`) that allows users to update their credentials (username/password or API token) and server region without removing and re-adding the integration. This is particularly useful when credentials change or a user wants to switch server regions.

To avoid duplicating credential validation logic between reauth and reconfigure, both flows now share a common `_async_step_credentials` helper method.

Marks `reconfiguration-flow` as done in `quality_scale.yaml`.

commits.message:
growatt_server: implement reconfiguration flow

Adds reconfiguration flow so users can update credentials (username/password
or API token) and server region without removing and re-adding the integration.
Refactors reauth into a shared _async_step_credentials helper to eliminate
duplication between reconfigure and reauth flows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

growatt_server: remove committed translations/en.json (gitignored)

translations/en.json is auto-generated by CI and gitignored; it should
not be committed to the repository.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

growatt_server: address reconfiguration-flow PR review comments

- Tighten _async_step_credentials entry parameter type from Any to ConfigEntry
- Add MagicMock type annotations to reconfigure test fixture parameters
- Assert specific error codes (invalid_auth/cannot_connect) in error recovery tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

growatt_server: address elupus review comments on reconfiguration flow tests

- Remove redundant snapshot assertion from test_reconfigure_password_success
- Replace `if login_return_value is not None` conditional with lambda-based
  side_effect parametrize in test_reconfigure_password_error_then_recovery
- Add MagicMock type annotations to all new reauth test fixture parameters
  for consistency with reconfigure tests
- Fix raw string keys "plant_id"/"name" → CONF_PLANT_ID/CONF_NAME
- Fix incorrect type annotation Exception → Exception | Callable[..., Any]
- Delete stale snapshots left over from removed snapshot assertion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

growatt_server: preserve non-secret field values on reauth/reconfigure error

When credential validation fails in the reauth or reconfigure flow, use
add_suggested_values_to_schema to re-populate non-secret fields (username,
region) with the last-submitted values. Without this, the form reverts to
the stored config entry values, requiring the user to re-enter any changes
they were trying to make.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

growatt_server: replace snapshot assertions with explicit assertions in tests

Replace all syrupy snapshot assertions with explicit dict and error
assertions in the config flow tests, as requested by reviewers.

- Remove SnapshotAssertion/props imports and snapshot fixture params
- Replace `entry.data == snapshot` with explicit dicts built from
  known constants and parametrize values
- Remove `result == snapshot(exclude=props("data_schema"))` on initial
  form shows (type/step_id already asserted above)
- Replace error-form snapshots with `result["errors"] == {"base": expected_error}`
- Refactor password error parametrize to use lambda side_effect +
  expected_error param, removing the if/return_value conditional
- Add expected_error to token error parametrize (both reauth and reconfigure)
- Clear the now-empty test_config_flow.ambr snapshot file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

growatt_server: remove empty test_config_flow snapshot file

No snapshot assertions remain in the config flow tests, so the file
is no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Add coordinator to Anthropic for availability check
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
Add Data Update Coordinator for availability check of Anthropic API.
The check is performed after 12 hours since the last successful response from API or after 1 minute if the last response was unsuccessful. Non-connectivity API errors are considered successful if received during conversation or ai task, but not during the automatic check.
This is an alternative approach for implementation of #164362 but using the coordinator.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a DataUpdateCoordinator-based availability mechanism for the Anthropic integration so entities can be marked unavailable when the API connectivity is degraded, with adaptive polling intervals for re-checks.

**Changes:**
- Add an `AnthropicCoordinator` (with a dynamic refresh interval) to periodically verify API availability and drive entity availability.
- Switch Anthropic entities to `CoordinatorEntity` and update conversation error handling to update coordinator state on success/failure.
- Add/adjust tests around coordinator-driven availability behavior and update repairs flow to use the coordinator’s client.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/anthropic/__init__.py` | Replaces direct client setup/validation with coordinator creation + first refresh and stores it in `entry.runtime_data`. |
| `homeassistant/components/anthropic/coordinator.py` | Adds the coordinator implementation, dynamic intervals, and logging for availability transitions. |
| `homeassistant/components/anthropic/entity.py` | Converts entities to `CoordinatorEntity` and ties API errors/successes to coordinator availability state. |
| `homeassistant/components/anthropic/repairs.py` | Updates repairs flow to access the Anthropic client via `entry.runtime_data.client`. |
| `homeassistant/components/anthropic/quality_scale.yaml` | Marks `entity-unavailable` and `log-when-unavailable` as done. |
| `tests/components/anthropic/test_coordinator.py` | Adds coordinator-focused tests for availability, reauth behavior, and retry-after handling. |
| `tests/components/anthropic/test_conversation.py` | Removes duplicated auth-reauth test now covered in coordinator tests and cleans imports. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

This PR improves naming consistency for the `system_log` integration's service actions by updating action names and descriptions. The changes align with Home Assistant conventions by including the domain name ("system log") in action names (since the domain prefix is dropped in the Frontend UI) and ensuring descriptions use consistent third-person singular verb forms.

**Changes:**
- Updated the "clear" service name from "Clear" to "Clear system log" and description to "Deletes all system log entries."
- Updated the "write" service name from "Write" to "Write to system log" and description to "Writes a system log entry."

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the London Underground integration to reflect current TfL line offerings by adding new selectable services and removing a no-longer-supported one, alongside a dependency bump to support the updated line set.

**Changes:**
- Bump `london-tube-status` dependency from `0.5` to `0.7`.
- Update the selectable line list by adding **Tram** and **IFS Cloud Cable Car**, and removing **London Overground**.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/london_underground/manifest.json` | Updates the pinned `london-tube-status` dependency version. |
| `homeassistant/components/london_underground/const.py` | Adjusts the available line names used for UI selection and YAML validation. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2)</summary>

**homeassistant/components/london_underground/const.py:24**
* Keep legacy YAML imports working for removed lines: `sensor.PLATFORM_SCHEMA` uses `TUBE_LINES` for `vol.In(...)`, so removing a legacy value like "London Overground" here will cause existing `configuration.yaml` configs to fail validation before the import flow can warn and strip it. Consider splitting UI-selectable lines from YAML-accepted legacy lines (or otherwise allowing legacy values only for import/migration) so users aren’t hard-broken during the YAML-to-config-entry transition.
```
TUBE_LINES = [
    "Bakerloo",
    "Central",
    "Circle",
    "District",
    "DLR",
    "Elizabeth line",
    "Hammersmith & City",
    "Jubilee",
    "Metropolitan",
    "Northern",
    "Piccadilly",
    "Victoria",
    "Waterloo & City",
```
**homeassistant/components/london_underground/const.py:24**
* Document the user-facing impact as a breaking change (entity removal): removing "London Overground" from the selectable lines means any existing `sensor.london_underground_london_overground` entity will disappear, and existing entries/options may need migration. Please fill in the PR template’s Breaking change section (or add release-note details) so users know what changes and why.
```
TUBE_LINES = [
    "Bakerloo",
    "Central",
    "Circle",
    "District",
    "DLR",
    "Elizabeth line",
    "Hammersmith & City",
    "Jubilee",
    "Metropolitan",
    "Northern",
    "Piccadilly",
    "Victoria",
    "Waterloo & City",
```
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

Thanks for bumping the dependency! However, could you please update the PR description to contain at least one (or multiple) of the following:

- A link to the release notes of this package version, and all versions in between.
- A link to the changelog of this package.
- A link to a Git(Hub) diff/compare view from the current version to the bumped version.

This allows us to review upstream changes, which is needed to decide this change is working as intended and/or if we can include it in, for example, a patch release of Home Assistant.

Thanks already! 👍

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

Thanks @prpr19xx 👍 

Copilot review will not suggest creating tests files. Just points out that you should test it. Tests can be added in a follow up PR

Add Tram and IFS Cloud Cable Car status

Also remove defunct London Overground entity
Reinstate defunct London Overground entity to stop tests failing

title: Fix Proxmox VE backup status sensor false positive due to case mismatch
body:
## Proposed change

Fix the Proxmox VE backup status binary sensor always reporting "Problem" even when backups succeed. The root cause is a case-sensitivity bug: the `STATUS_OK` constant in `const.py` was set to `"ok"` (lowercase), but the Proxmox API returns `"OK"` (uppercase) in the task status field. The comparison `data.backups[0]["status"] != STATUS_OK` therefore always evaluated to `True`, causing the sensor to permanently report a problem state.

The fix changes `STATUS_OK` from `"ok"` to `"OK"` to match the actual Proxmox API response. The test snapshot was also updated from expecting `'state': 'on'` (problem) to `'state': 'off'` (no problem), since the test fixture already correctly had `"status": "OK"`.

reviews.body:
Hello @irishpadres,

When attempting to inspect the commits of your pull request for CLA signature status among all authors we encountered commit(s) which were not linked to a GitHub account, thus not allowing us to determine their status(es).

The commits that are missing a linked GitHub account are the following:


- [`c1e4bb2bdd24053c2feb7f0c71fcc7b6c8c6b78b`](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/pull/167069/commits/c1e4bb2bdd24053c2feb7f0c71fcc7b6c8c6b78b) - This commit has something that looks like an email address (root@tahoe.kobrien.net). Maybe try linking that to GitHub?.


Unfortunately, **we are unable to accept this pull request until this situation is corrected.**

Here are your options:

1. If you had an email address set for the commit that simply wasn't linked to your GitHub account you can link that email now and it will retroactively apply to your commits. The simplest way to do this is to click the link to one of the above commits and look for a blue question mark in a blue circle in the top left. Hovering over that bubble will show you what email address you used. Clicking on that button will take you to your email address settings on GitHub. Just add the email address on that page and you're all set. GitHub has more information about this option [in their help center](https://help.github.com/articles/setting-your-email-in-git/#commits-on-github-arent-linking-to-my-account).

2. If you didn't use an email address at all, it was an invalid email, or it's one you can't link to your GitHub, you will need to change the authorship information of the commit and your global Git settings so this doesn't happen again going forward. GitHub provides some great instructions on how to change your authorship information [in their help center](https://help.github.com/articles/setting-your-email-in-git/).
   * If you only made a single commit you should be able to run
      ```
      git commit --amend --author="Author Name <email@address.com>"
      ```

## Pull request overview

This pull request fixes a case-sensitivity bug in the Proxmox VE integration where the backup status binary sensor was always reporting "Problem" regardless of actual backup status. The root cause was a mismatch between the `STATUS_OK` constant defined as `"ok"` (lowercase) and the actual Proxmox API response of `"OK"` (uppercase).

**Changes:**
- Changed `STATUS_OK` constant from lowercase `"ok"` to uppercase `"OK"` to match the Proxmox API response
- Updated test snapshot to expect `'state': 'off'` instead of `'state': 'on'` for successful backup status

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/proxmoxve/const.py | Fixed case mismatch in STATUS_OK constant to match API response |
| tests/components/proxmoxve/snapshots/test_binary_sensor.ambr | Updated backup status binary sensor expected state from on (problem) to off (no problem) |

Hi @irishpadres

It seems you haven't yet signed a CLA. Please do so [here](https://home-assistant.io/developers/cla_sign_start/?pr=home-assistant/core#167069).

Once you do that we will be able to review and accept this pull request.

Thanks!

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
Thanks for patching this, @irishpadres!

commits.message:
Fix Proxmox VE backup status sensor false positive

The STATUS_OK constant was "ok" (lowercase) but the Proxmox API
returns "OK" (uppercase) for successful task status, causing the
backup status binary sensor to always report a problem.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request fixes the Tesla Fleet `charge_state_charge_current_request` number entity to accept either `VEHICLE_CHARGING_CMDS` or `VEHICLE_CMDS` scopes, matching the behavior of the `set_charging_amps` API command and preventing the entity from being incorrectly treated as read-only when an application has the broader `VEHICLE_CMDS` scope.

**Changes:**
- Updated the `charge_state_charge_current_request` entity scopes to include both `VEHICLE_CHARGING_CMDS` and `VEHICLE_CMDS`, making it consistent with the `charge_state_charge_limit_soc` entity

commits.message: Fix Tesla Fleet charge current scope handling

## Pull request overview

This PR reintroduces the Dropbox integration into Home Assistant Core (reverting the prior removal), restoring its OAuth2-based config flow and Dropbox-backed backup agent support.

**Changes:**
- Add the `dropbox` integration implementation (OAuth2 application credentials + config flow + backup agent).
- Add a full Dropbox test suite covering setup/unload, config flow, and backup agent API behavior.
- Re-register Dropbox in generated registries and dependency/typing infrastructure (requirements, strict-typing, mypy, CODEOWNERS).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 19 out of 22 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/__init__.py` | Sets up the integration, creates the Dropbox API client, handles auth/not-ready, and registers backup agent reload listeners. |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/backup.py` | Implements the Dropbox `BackupAgent` (list/get/download/upload/delete) and backup-agent listener registration. |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/config_flow.py` | Adds OAuth2 config flow and reauth steps for Dropbox. |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/application_credentials.py` | Provides a custom OAuth2 implementation (PKCE + Dropbox-specific authorize params). |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/auth.py` | Adds Auth adapters for config-entry OAuth2 session and config-flow fixed token usage. |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/const.py` | Defines domain/constants and the hass.data key for agent listeners. |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/manifest.json` | Declares integration metadata and dependency on `python-dropbox-api`. |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/strings.json` | Adds config-flow strings and exception translation key(s). |
| `homeassistant/components/dropbox/quality_scale.yaml` | Declares integration quality scale rule statuses. |
| `tests

Thanks!

Comment addressed.

**.claude/agents/github-pr-reviewer.md:4**

Thanks!

Side note: These files don't appear to be marked as core team owned. At least I wasn't notified. I think they should be owned by core team.

title: Fix websocket calling `async_release_notes` in update component although unavailable
body:

<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

When the release notes are fetched by the frontend via the websocket command, an error is returned if the entity was not found or if the entity doesn't support release notes. reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a bug where the websocket command to fetch release notes would call `async_release_notes()` on unavailable entities, potentially causing integrations to raise exceptions when they try to access runtime data that is not available. The fix adds an availability check before calling the async method and returns an appropriate error to the client.

**Changes:**
- Added an availability check in the websocket handler for release notes requests to prevent calling `async_release_notes()` on unavailable entities
- Refactored the `MockUpdateEntity` test utility class from the test file to a shared `common.py` module to avoid duplication
- Added a comprehensive test case for the newly added availability check

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/update/__init__.py | Added availability check before calling `async_release_notes()` in the websocket handler |
| tests/components/update/test_init.py | Imported `MockUpdateEntity` from common module, updated test_update to use base `UpdateEntity` class, and added new test case for unavailable entities |
Thanks!

commits.message: Fix websocket calling async_release_notes in update component when unavailable

## Pull request overview

This pull request adds a new action `html5.dismiss_message` to the HTML5 integration, designed to work with notify entities, and deprecates the older `html5.dismiss` action which required specifying targets as a list.

**Changes:**
- Added new `html5.dismiss_message` action that works with notify entities to dismiss HTML5 notifications with optional tag filtering
- Deprecated the `html5.dismiss` action with appropriate deprecation warnings
- Added service registration, schema validation, and user-facing strings for the new action
- Added comprehensive tests for the new functionality and deprecation warnings

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/html5/services.py | Registers the new dismiss_message service with entity service schema |
| homeassistant/components/html5/notify.py | Implements dismiss_notification method and adds deprecation warning to async_dismiss |
| homeassistant/components/html5/issue.py | Adds deprecated_dismiss_action_call function to create deprecation issues |
| homeassistant/components/html5/strings.json | Adds service description and issue text for the new action |
| homeassistant/components/html5/services.yaml | Adds service definition with tag field anchor for reusability |
| homeassistant/components/html5/icons.json | Adds icon for dismiss_message action |
| tests/components/html5/test_notify.py | Adds tests for dismiss_message action and refactors deprecation tests |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

title: Improve date action naming consistency
body:
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

Aligns the datetime integration's action description with the established naming patterns used across Home Assistant.

The description was "Sets the date." which didn't reference the entity type with the proper article. This PR up dates it to "Sets the value of a date." for consistency. The action name "Set date" already included the entity type and remains unchanged.

<details>
<summary>Action naming style guide</summary>

# Action/Service Naming & Style Guide

This guide defines the naming patterns for triggers, conditions, and actions (services) in Home Assistant integrations. The trigger and condition patterns are already established and consistent across integrations. The action patterns are derived from those to bring consistency across all three.

## Entity Type

Each integration uses a consistent **entity type** across all its triggers, conditions, and actions. This is the human-friendly name for the type of device.

Examples: "light", "fan", "cover", "lock", "alarm", "media player", "vacuum cleaner", "climate-control device", "lawn mower"

The same entity type must be used consistently within an integration. It should match what is already used in its triggers and conditions.

## Names

### Triggers (established)

Pattern: `[Entity type] [past tense event]`

The entity type comes first, followed by the past tense event description.

Examples:

- "Light turned on"
- "Climate-control device started cooling"
- "Lawn mower started mowing"
- "Lock locked"

### Conditions (established)

Pattern: `[Entity type] is [state]`

The entity type comes first, followed by "is" and the state.

Examples:

- "Light is on"
- "Climate-control device is cooling"
- "Lawn mower is mowing"
- "Lock is locked"

Value-based variant: `[Entity type] [property]`

- "Climate-control device target temperature"

### Actions (proposed)

Pattern: `[Verb] [entity type]` or `[Verb] [entity type] [detail]`

The entity type should always be included. Since actions are imperative commands, the verb comes first (unlike triggers/conditions which are statements and lead with the entity type).

**Power/state actions**: `[Verb] [entity type]`

- "Turn on light"
- "Toggle fan"
- "Pause media player"
- "Lock lock"
- "Disarm alarm"

**Property actions**: `Set [entity type] [property]`

- "Set fan speed"
- "Set cover position"
- "Set media player volume"

**Other actions**: Entity type in the most natural position

- "Open cover"
- "Locate vacuum cleaner"
- "Return lawn mower to dock"
- "Increase fan speed"
- "Select media player source"
- "Send command to vacuum cleaner"

## Descriptions

### Triggers (established)

Pattern: "Triggers after one or more [entity type plural] [present tense verb phrase]."

Examples:

- "Triggers after one or more lights turn on."
- "Triggers after one or more lawn mowers start mowing."
- "Triggers after one or more locks lock."

### Conditions (established)

Pattern: "Tests if one or more [entity type plural] are [state]."

Examples:

- "Tests if one or more lights are on."
- "Tests if one or more climate-control devices are cooling."
- "Tests if one or more locks are locked."

Value-based variant: "Tests the [property] of one or more [entity type plural]."

### Actions (proposed)

Pattern: "[Third person verb phrase] a [entity type]."

Always mention the entity type. Use the indefinite article "a"/"an" (not "the"). No unnecessary filler words.

**Device actions** (the verb directly applies to the device): `[Verb]s a [entity type].`

- "Turns on a light."
- "Toggles a fan on/off."
- "Locks a lock."
- "Locates a vacuum cleaner."

**Task/activity actions** (the verb applies to a task the device performs): `[Verb]s a [entity type]'s [task/activity].`

Some actions control an activity or task that a device performs, rather than the device itself. For example, starting a vacuum cleaner starts its cleaning task — it does not power on the device (that is `turn_on`). In these cases, reference both the entity type and the task to avoid ambiguity.

- "Starts or resumes a vacuum cleaner's cleaning task."
- "Pauses a vacuum cleaner's current task."
- "Stops a vacuum cleaner's current task."
- "Starts a lawn mower's mowing task."
- "Pauses a lawn mower's current task."

**Property actions**: `Sets the [property] of a [entity type].`

- "Sets the speed of a fan."
- "Sets the position of a cover."
- "Sets the volume level of a media player."

**Other actions**: Include "a [entity type]" naturally

- "Opens a cover."
- "Returns a lawn mower to its dock."
- "Sends a command to a vacuum cleaner."

</details>

## Type of change
<!--
  What type of change does your PR introduce to Home Assistant?
  NOTE: Please, check only 1! box!
  If your PR requires multiple boxes to be checked, you'll most likely need to
  split it into multiple PRs. This makes things easier and faster to code review.
-->

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New integration (thank you!)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality to an existing integration)
- [ ] Deprecation (breaking change to happen in the future)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [x] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information
<!--
  Details are important, and help maintainers processing your PR.
  Please be sure to fill out additional details, if applicable.
-->

- This PR fixes or closes issue: fixes #
- This PR is related to issue:
- Link to documentation pull request:
- Link to developer documentation pull request:
- Link to frontend pull request:

## Checklist
<!--
  Put an `x` in the boxes that apply. You can also fill these out after
  creating the PR. If you're unsure about any of them, don't hesitate to ask.
  We're here to help! This is simply a reminder of what we are going to look
  for before merging your code.

  AI tools are welcome, but contributors are responsible for *fully*
  understanding the code before submitting a PR.
-->

- [x] I understand the code I am submitting and can explain how it works.
- [x] The code change is tested and works locally.
- [x] Local tests pass. **Your PR cannot be merged unless tests pass**
- [x] There is no commented out code in this PR.
- [x] I have followed the [development checklist][dev-checklist]
- [x] I have followed the [perfect PR recommendations][perfect-pr]
- [x] The code has been formatted using Ruff (`ruff format homeassistant tests`)
- [x] Tests have been added to verify that the new code works.
- [x] Any generated code has been carefully reviewed for correctness and compliance with project standards.

If user exposed functionality or configuration variables are added/changed:

- [ ] Documentation added/updated for [www.home-assistant.io][docs-repository]

If the code communicates with devices, web services, or third-party tools:

- [ ] The [manifest file][manifest-docs] has all fields filled out correctly.
      Updated and included derived files by running: `python3 -m script.hassfest`.
- [ ] New or updated dependencies have been added to `requirements_all.txt`.
      Updated by running `python3 -m script.gen_requirements_all`.
- [ ] For the updated dependencies a diff between library versions and ideally a link to the changelog/release notes is added to the PR description.

  This project is very active and we have a high turnover of pull requests.

  Unfortunately, the number of incoming pull requests is higher than what our
  reviewers can review and merge so there is a long backlog of pull requests

-->

title: Prevent invalid phase count state in nrgkick
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Breaking change
<!--
  If your PR contains a breaking change for existing users, it is important
  to tell them what breaks, how to make it work again and why we did this.
  This piece of text is published with the release notes, so it helps if you
  write it towards our users, not us.
  Note: Remove this section if this PR is NOT a breaking change.
-->


## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

After a phase count change, the device first confirms the new phase count with the returned value, but then briefly returns a phase count of 0 for the next 1-2 seconds while it's changing the phase count. With the delayed refresh and entity updates happening only every 30 seconds, this transient phase count is reported for a long time, causing bad UX and confusion.

Therefore, the new approach now takes the confirmed device value and considers a phase count of 0 as illegal and doesn't use this, as charging phases can only be 1 (or 2, 3 depending on the connection). This also reflects the behavior of the official NRGkick app, which also doesn't show a phase count of 0 in the brief transition phase.

This makes the device-confirmed new phase count immediately visible.
(Note: when the device is not charging currently or not connected, the returned phase count is also the configured value, and not 0).

## Type of change

## Pull request overview

This PR ensures trigger platforms are registered when a trigger is first used, so the trigger-description system can discover and load the corresponding `triggers.yaml` metadata.

**Changes:**
- Register the trigger platform from within `_async_get_trigger_platform()` to populate `hass.data[TRIGGERS]` on-demand.
- Make `_register_trigger_platform()` idempotent to avoid re-registering existing triggers and to reduce misleading debug logs.
- Add a test covering trigger registration and subscriber notification behavior when a trigger platform is first accessed.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/helpers/trigger.py` | Registers trigger platforms on-demand and makes platform registration idempotent. |
| `tests/helpers/test_trigger.py` | Adds coverage verifying registration side effects and subscriber notifications. |
Looks good!

## Pull request overview

This pull request fixes a StopIteration error in the ista EcoTrend coordinator and updates library API calls to match a pyecotrend-ista library update.

**Changes:**
- Refactors the `get_details()` method in the coordinator to use a nested dictionary comprehension instead of `next()`, eliminating the potential StopIteration error
- Removes the `_LOGGER` argument from three `PyEcotrendIsta` instantiations to match the updated library API (version 3.4.0)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/ista_ecotrend/coordinator.py | Refactors dictionary comprehension to avoid StopIteration error |
| homeassistant/components/ista_ecotrend/config_flow.py | Removes `_LOGGER` argument from PyEcotrendIsta constructor calls |
| homeassistant/components/ista_ecotrend/__init__.py | Removes `_LOGGER` argument from PyEcotrendIsta constructor call |
Thanks @tr4nt0r 👍

## Pull request overview

This PR ensures condition platforms are registered when a condition is used, so condition descriptions (from `conditions.yaml`) can be discovered/loaded even if the integration hasn’t been set up as a top-level component yet.

**Changes:**
- Register the condition platform during `_async_get_condition_platform()` lookups to populate `hass.data[CONDITIONS]` and notify subscribers.
- Make `_register_condition_platform()` idempotent by only registering and notifying for newly-seen condition keys.
- Add a regression test verifying on-demand registration and subscriber notifications.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/helpers/condition.py` | Registers condition platforms on-demand and avoids duplicate condition-key registrations/notifications. |
| `tests/helpers/test_condition.py` | Adds test coverage to assert on-demand registration populates `CONDITIONS` and emits a single subscriber event. |
Thanks!

- Update hassfest tests for triggers and conditions to cover fields without descriptions.
- Adjust hassfest validation comments/docs to reflect the new behavior.

title: Add support for energy statistics in waterfurnace integration
body:
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

The `waterfurnace` integration exposes sensors for immediate power usage, but not long term energy use. The however library exposes methods for fetching historic energy usage, which can be used to populate energy statistics. This adds a new energy coordinator that will load data for the last day, and refresh any energy use every 2 hours.

## Type of change
<!--
  What type of change does your PR introduce to Home Assistant?
  NOTE: Please, check only 1! box!
  If your PR requires multiple boxes to be checked, you'll most likely need to
  split it into multiple PRs. This makes things easier and faster to code review.
-->

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New integration (thank you!)
- [x] New feature (which adds functionality to an existing integration)
- [ ] Deprecation (breaking change to happen in the future)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [ ] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information
<!--
  Details are important, and help maintainers processing your PR.
  Please be sure to fill out additional details, if applicable.
-->

- Adds a `WaterFurnaceDeviceData` class for holding both coordinators, sensor data and energy data
  - A separate coordinator was chosen because we only want to fetch data every 2 hours. Additionally, this helps prevent bugs or issues with fetching energy data from blocking the more important real-time sensor data (we can always backfill energy data, but real-time sensor data is lost if not collected).
- Adds a new `WaterFurnaceEnergyCooridnator`
  - Fetches new data from the energy specific endpoint every 2 hours.
  - On launch, it will try to load the last day of data or however much data may be missing if it had already been running.

I've been testing this for the past few weeks to try and find any potential edge cases that may be missed by tests without much issue (after the first few days and bug fixes at least :smile:)

Note: It's possible to fetch longer historical data, up to 13 months, however the PR was getting large enough. My goal is to do a follow-up PR as soon as this is merged, with the hope of landing both changes in the same release so we have back-filling out of the box, otherwise back-filling may get complicated.

- Link to documentation pull request: https://github.com/home-assistant/home-assistant.io/pull/44350

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds long-term energy statistics support to the `waterfurnace` integration by introducing a dedicated energy coordinator that periodically fetches hourly usage and writes it to the recorder as external statistics (for the Energy Dashboard).

**Changes:**
- Introduce `WaterFurnaceEnergyCoordinator` and a `WaterFurnaceDeviceData` container holding both realtime + energy coordinators per device.
- Write hourly kWh usage into recorder statistics on a 2-hour interval, with initial backfill behavior.
- Add/adjust tests to validate statistics insertion, behavior across polls, and multi-device coordinator/statistic IDs.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/waterfurnace/coordinator.py | Adds energy coordinator that fetches energy history and inserts external statistics. |
| homeassistant/components/waterfurnace/__init__.py | Sets up both coordinators per device and stores them in `entry.runtime_data`. |
| homeassistant/components/waterfurnace/sensor.py | Switches sensor setup to use the per-device realtime coordinator from `WaterFurnaceDeviceData`. |
| homeassistant/components/waterfurnace/const.py | Adds `ENERGY_UPDATE_INTERVAL` (2 hours). |
| homeassistant/components/waterfurnace/manifest.json | Declares a dependency on `recorder` for statistics support. |
| tests/components/waterfurnace/test_statistics.py | New test coverage for statistics insertion, skipping current hour, resume behavior, and TZ handling. |
| tests/components/waterfurnace/test_init.py | Updates setup/reload expectations and adds coordinator creation tests. |
| tests/components/waterfurnace/test_config_flow.py | Updates tests to include recorder fixture where needed. |
| tests/components/waterfurnace/conftest.py | Adds default `WFNoDataError` behavior for `get_energy_dat

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

commits.message:
Use WaterFurnaceDeviceData wrapper

Introduces WaterFurnaceDeviceData dataclass to hold per-device
coordinators, preparing for additional coordinator types.

Add energy coordinator for waterfurnace

Adds WaterFurnaceEnergyCoordinator that periodically fetches hourly
energy data from the API and inserts external statistics for the
Energy Dashboard.
Make message more abgious to account for edge cases

Move recorder to after_dependencies

Cleanup unnecessary mock_recorder in tests
Use freeze_time fixture for time, and share the time as a file wide constant
Check mock client methods are called

title: Add --draft flag to raise-pull-request agent PR creation command

## Pull request overview

Updates the SmartThings integration dependency to pick up the upstream `pySmartThings` patch release, ensuring Home Assistant ships with the latest compatible library version.

**Changes:**
- Bump `pysmartthings` from `3.7.2` to `3.7.3` for the SmartThings integration.
- Sync the version bump across `manifest.json`, `requirements_all.txt`, and `requirements_test_all.txt`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/smartthings/manifest.json` | Updates the SmartThings integration pinned requirement to `pysmartthings==3.7.3`. |
| `requirements_all.txt` | Updates the global requirements pin for SmartThings to `3.7.3`. |
| `requirements_test_all.txt` | Updates the test requirements pin for SmartThings to `3.7.3`. |

## Pull request overview

This PR fixes spelling and capitalization issues in user-facing strings of the `wiz` (WiZ lights) integration. These corrections improve the quality of user-facing error and configuration messages by fixing grammatical errors and standardizing capitalization conventions.

**Changes:**
- Fixed "Can not" to "Cannot" in the bulb connection error message
- Removed unnecessary capitalization of "Platform" in the "no_wiz_light" error message
- Changed "IP Address" to "IP address" in the configuration description to follow sentence-case conventions

title: Migrate tilt_ble to use runtime_data
body:
## Proposed change

Migrate the tilt_ble integration from storing data in `hass.data` to using `entry.runtime_data`.

This adds a typed `TiltBLEConfigEntry` type alias (`ConfigEntry[PassiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator]`) and simplifies the unload logic by removing manual `hass.data` cleanup.

This is a step toward meeting the Bronze quality scale requirements for the `runtime-data` rule.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Migrates the `tilt_ble` integration to store its coordinator on the config entry via `entry.runtime_data` instead of `hass.data`, aligning the integration with Home Assistant’s runtime-data quality scale direction.

**Changes:**
- Add a typed `TiltBLEConfigEntry` alias (`ConfigEntry[PassiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator]`) to formalize the entry’s runtime data type.
- Store the `PassiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator` on `entry.runtime_data` during setup and read it from there in the sensor platform.
- Simplify unload logic by removing the now-unnecessary `hass.data` cleanup.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/tilt_ble/__init__.py` | Introduces `TiltBLEConfigEntry`, assigns the coordinator to `entry.runtime_data`, and removes `hass.data` teardown on unload. |
| `homeassistant/components/tilt_ble/sensor.py` | Switches sensor setup to use the typed config entry and fetch the coordinator from `entry.runtime_data`. |
Looks good

commits.message:
Use runtime_data in tilt_ble integration

Migrate the tilt_ble integration from storing data in hass.data to using
entry.runtime_data. This adds a typed TiltBLEConfigEntry type alias and
simplifies the unload logic by removing manual hass.data cleanup.
Fix ruff format: single-line function signatures

## Pull request overview

Migrates the NuHeat integration to use `ConfigEntry.runtime_data` for per-entry runtime objects instead of `hass.data`, aligning NuHeat with current Home Assistant integration patterns.

**Changes:**
- Store the NuHeat thermostat object and `NuHeatCoordinator` in `entry.runtime_data` via a new `NuHeatData` dataclass.
- Update the climate platform setup to read runtime objects from `config_entry.runtime_data` using a typed `NuHeatConfigEntry` alias.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/nuheat/__init__.py | Introduces `NuHeatData` + `NuHeatConfigEntry` and stores coordinator/thermostat on `entry.runtime_data`; removes `hass.data` storage/cleanup. |
| homeassistant/components/nuheat/climate.py | Switches platform setup to retrieve thermostat/coordinator from `config_entry.runtime_data` and updates typing accordingly. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

## Pull request overview

This PR corrects the SNMP test suite’s DNS-avoidance patch by patching the intended `socket` resolver method (follow-up to #166604).

**Changes:**
- Rename the autouse fixture to `patch_getaddrinfo`.
- Patch `socket.getaddrinfo` (instead of `socket.gethostbyname`) to avoid DNS lookups during SNMP tests.

## Pull request overview

This PR corrects the spelling of two words in the `kitchen_sink` component's strings.json file. The text "Can not re-heat the tea at this time" is corrected to "Cannot reheat the tea at this time", which follows proper English spelling conventions.

**Changes:**
- Corrects "Can not" to "Cannot" (proper single-word form)
- Corrects "re-heat" to "reheat" (non-hyphenated form)

title: Add sensor platform for OpenDisplay
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->

## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
Adds the `sensor` platform for the OpenDisplay integration. Sensors are driven by passive BLE advertisement data.

Three sensors are added:
- Battery (%): only created for battery- and solar-powered devices
- Battery voltage (mV): only created for battery- and solar-powered devices and disabled by default
- Temperature (°C): chip temperature, always created but disabled by default

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a `sensor` platform to the existing `opendisplay` integration, using passive BLE advertisements as the data source, alongside the supporting coordinator/entity scaffolding and dependency bump.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a passive BLE `OpenDisplayCoordinator` and shared `OpenDisplayEntity` base to drive entities from advertisements.
- Adds new OpenDisplay sensor entities (temperature, battery %, battery voltage) plus test coverage for sensor behavior.
- Bumps `py-opendisplay` to `5.4.0` and updates integration quality scale markers accordingly.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/opendisplay/__init__.py` | Adds sensor platform forwarding and coordinator startup; adjusts unload behavior. |
| `homeassistant/components/opendisplay/coordinator.py` | New passive BLE coordinator to parse manufacturer advertisement payloads. |
| `homeassistant/components/opendisplay/entity.py` | New base entity providing `DeviceInfo` and unique IDs for OpenDisplay entities. |
| `homeassistant/components/opendisplay/sensor.py` | New sensor platform + entity descriptions for temperature/battery/voltage. |
| `homeassistant/components/opendisplay/manifest.json` | Updates dependency pin to `py-opendisplay==5.4.0`. |
| `homeassistant/components/opendisplay/quality_scale.yaml` | Marks entity-related quality scale requirements as done now that entities exist. |
| `requirements_all.txt` | Updates global requirements pin for `py-opendisplay` to `5.4.0`. |
| `requirements_test_all.txt` | Updates test requirements pin for `py-opendisplay` to `5.4.0`. |
| `tests/components/opendisplay/__init__.py` | Updates test fixture config structure for the new `py-opendisplay` version. |
| `tests/components/opendisplay/test_sensor.py` | Adds tests for sensor creation and

## Pull request overview

This PR fixes lingering tasks in condition and trigger tests by adding cleanup code to properly reset the sun entity component after tests complete. The changes reference issue #166758 about fixing asyncio loop scopes for pytest fixtures. This is a code quality improvement that prevents lingering asynchronous tasks from interfering with subsequent tests.

**Changes:**
- Added `_async_reset()` calls to clean up sun entity component in 4 test functions in test_trigger.py
- Added `_async_reset()` calls to clean up sun entity component in 4 test functions in test_condition.py

### Reviewed changes

| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/helpers/test_trigger.py | Added cleanup code to reset sun component in 4 test functions |

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Disables two high-churn, secondary entities by default in the `sunricher_dali` integration to satisfy the Gold-tier `entity-disabled-by-default` quality scale rule.

**Changes:**
- Disable the energy sensor and instantaneous motion binary sensor by default via `_attr_entity_registry_enabled_default = False`.
- Update binary sensor and sensor snapshot/behavior tests to explicitly enable disabled-by-default entities before asserting on their state.
- Convert the `init_integration` test fixture to an async generator (`yield`) so the `_PLATFORMS` patch remains active across config entry reloads.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/sunricher_dali/sensor.py` | Disables the energy diagnostic sensor by default. |
| `homeassistant/components/sunricher_dali/binary_sensor.py` | Disables the instantaneous motion binary sensor by default. |
| `homeassistant/components/sunricher_dali/quality_scale.yaml` | Marks `entity-disabled-by-default` as done. |
| `tests/components/sunricher_dali/conftest.py` | Uses `yield` in `init_integration` to keep platform patch active during reloads. |
| `tests/components/sunricher_dali/test_sensor.py` | Enables disabled-by-default sensor entities for snapshots and energy sensor tests. |
| `tests/components/sunricher_dali/test_binary_sensor.py` | Enables disabled-by-default binary sensor entities for snapshots and motion sensor tests. |
</details>

I don't consder energy a secondary entity, and I think it's fine for the motion detection to be there, as that is the main feature of the device right?

So IMO you can just set it to exempt

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 1 out of 1 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

commits.message:
Disable noisy entities by default in sunricher_dali

Disable the energy sensor and motion binary sensor by default to
conserve system resources. The energy sensor is diagnostic data and
the motion sensor is a noisy counterpart to the occupancy sensor.

Update tests to enable these entities before testing their behavior.

Mark entity-disabled-by-default as exempt in sunricher_dali

Revert entity disabling changes per reviewer feedback. Energy is
valuable device data and motion detection is a core device feature.
With only a handful of entities, nothing needs to be disabled.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for the Netatmo Doorbell (NDB) camera type in the webhook connection handling and adds corresponding test coverage. The NDB was already present in test fixtures and supported in the camera entity code (e.g., `supported_features` already handles `NDB` type), but was missing from the `CAMERA_CONNECTION_WEBHOOKS` list and from parameterized camera tests.

**Changes:**

- Extends two existing parameterized test cases (`test_setup_component_with_webhook` and `test_camera_reconnect_webhook`) with NDB test data, introducing an `expected` parameter to handle the NDB's initial "idle" state (vs "streaming" for other cameras).

title: Validate set_system_mode params in code instead of by schema for Evohome
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

Move most of the validation of the `set_system_mode` service call parameters from a voluptuous schema to code.
 - permits use of `ServiceValidationError` instead of `vol.Invalid`

> This PR prepares this integration for config flow; specifically for multiple systems per user login.

Most evohome-compatible systems support the same system modes (although older systems do not support `AutoWithReset`). However, a minority of systems support a subset, or a distinct set of such modes.

Therefore, the evohome integration builds the service schemas dynamically when the integration is first loaded.

But evohome will soon support multiple systems (rather than simply the first system it finds), and each could have a different set of modes.

Thus, validation of the `set_system_mode` service call parameters is being moved; from a voluptuous schema, into code. Currently, this is done in **services.py**, but when controller service calls become entity-level services, a method in the corresponding entity class, `EvoController`, will invokes these checks.

A subsequent PR will migrate these services from domain- to entity-level service calls.

**Changes:**

- Adds translated `ServiceValidationError` mappings for invalid system modes and API failures.
- Adds/updates tests covering the new validation and translated errors.

Copilot reviewed 300 out of 941 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2)</summary>

**homeassistant/components/climate/services.yaml:1**
* Configure the service selectors to reference the attributes that provide the set of valid choices (e.g., `preset_modes` and `hvac_modes`) rather than the current `preset_mode`/entity state. As written, the UI is likely to be unable to offer a correct dropdown of options for these service fields.
```
# Describes the format for available climate services
```
**homeassistant/components/huum/diagnostics.py:1**
* Handle the case where `coordinator.data` is `None` before calling `to_dict()`. The previous implementation explicitly supported `None` data; without a guard this can raise an AttributeError when diagnostics are requested after a failed first refresh.
```
"""Diagnostics support for Huum."""
```
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

revert change (was raising all API failures as service failure)
remove rare modes in favour of custom_value
pull out exception handler to upper context
slight refactor will make upcoming PR smaller

title: Add Claude Code agent for PR creation
body:
## Proposed change

Add a Claude Code agent configuration file that automates the pull request creation process for the Home Assistant core repository. This agent:

- Gathers branch and commit information
- Runs code quality checks (ruff format, ruff check, hassfest)
- Executes integration tests
- Verifies the development checklist
- Determines the appropriate PR type and checkbox states
- Creates properly formatted PRs with correct checkbox handling

This helps contributors create well-formatted PRs that follow Home Assistant's PR guidelines and checklist requirements.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New integration (thank you!)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality to an existing integration)
- [ ] Deprecation (breaking change to happen in the future)

reviews.body:
I'm not sure if there's value in making it easier to automate making PRs in this project. You can see the number of pending PRs is high enough already.

I think it's a good thing to require a few manual steps before submitting a PR. This ensures that we're not wasting human time in reviewing entirely automated PRs. It also ensures that there's a human at the other end of the line who can respond to feedback or questions

Thanks for this @Bre77 !
While I agree that it may be good to have this to help users create higher quality PRs, @EmandM has a good point too.

I'll discuss this with the core team and get back here later.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds a Claude Code agent configuration file that automates pull request creation for the Home Assistant core repository. The agent guides through the complete PR workflow including code quality checks, test execution, checklist verification, and PR body formatting with proper checkbox states.

**Changes:**
- Adds a new Claude agent configuration file `.claude/agents/raise-pull-request.md` that provides step-by-step instructions for creating PRs automatically
- Includes command examples for git operations, testing, code quality checks, and GitHub CLI PR creation
- Defines logic for determining PR type, checkbox states, and proper formatting based on the official PR template

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 1 out of 1 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 1 out of 1 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `a528225540`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

Commented 1 concern but I think it is good to merge anyway, and address that separately in a follow-up PR.

Thanks @Bre77 !
Would it make sense to instruct the agent to create a draft PR, so the user takes the additional step to mark it as ready for review (after "I understand the code..." and "Any generated code...")?

commits.message:
Update raise-pull-request agent to preserve all PR template options

- Keep all "Type of change" options in PR body, only checking the selected one
- Add explicit placeholder names for each type checkbox
- Add instructions clarifying that all options must remain visible
- Update template note to explain checkbox handling for both sections

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix raise-pull-request agent to match actual PR template exactly

- Add Breaking change section at the start (removed if not applicable)
- Fix Type of change order to match template exactly
- Add missing "Deprecation" type option
- Add "(thank you!)" to New integration option
- Add conditional checklist sections for:
  - Documentation for user-exposed functionality
  - Manifest file validation
  - Requirements file updates
  - Dependency changelog links
- Leave "I understand the code" unchecked for user to verify
- Update checkbox placeholder names for clarity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revise PR title format and add examples

Updated PR title format to use release-note-style summary and provided examples for various types of changes.

Address PR review feedback for raise-pull-request agent

- Use prek instead of individual ruff/hassfest checks, abort on failure
- Replace git commit --amend and push --force with regular commit and push
- Read PR template from .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md instead of
  hardcoding it, to stay in sync with upstream changes

https://claude.ai/code/session_01E72e6USrWYaz9LKPiJcjzj

Merge pull request #972 from Bre77/claude/address-pr-160759-feedback-AFElu

Address PR review feedback for raise-pull-request agent

Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Abílio Costa <abmantis@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update code quality PR title examples

Replace examples that were actually bugfix/dependency changes with
real merged code quality PRs from the HA core repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove coverage flags from pytest command

Coverage generation isn't used in the PR workflow, so there's no
need for --cov and --cov-report flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Leave "tested and works locally" checkbox unchecked

This checkbox refers to manual testing by the contributor, not
unit tests. The "Local tests pass" checkbox covers unit tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clarify REQUIREMENTS_UPDATED variable logic

Make it clear that REQUIREMENTS_UPDATED requires both
MANIFEST_CHANGED to be true and the regeneration to succeed,
rather than defaulting to true when manifest didn't change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Preserve PR template text intact

The PR template says "DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template"
so the agent should keep all template text rather than selectively
removing comments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove explicit git push before PR creation

gh pr create pushes the branch automatically, so the separate
git push step is unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Fix remaining PR agent review issues

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds new diagnostic WAN status enum sensors to the UniFi integration (for gateway devices), enabled via an `aiounifi` dependency bump that exposes the required WAN fields.

**Changes:**
- Bump `aiounifi` from 88 → 90 across integration manifest and pinned requirements.
- Add factory-generated WAN status (`online`/`offline`) enum sensors for WAN1–WAN6, disabled by default and filtered per-device.
- Add `wan_status` enum state translations and a new unit test covering online→offline transitions.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/unifi/sensor.py` | Adds supported/value helpers plus a factory to create up to 6 WAN status enum sensors and registers them with existing entity descriptions. |
| `homeassistant/components/unifi/strings.json` | Adds translations for `wan_status` enum states (`online`/`offline`). |
| `homeassistant/components/unifi/manifest.json` | Pins UniFi integration dependency to `aiounifi==90`. |
| `requirements_all.txt` | Updates global pinned `aiounifi` version to 90. |
| `requirements_test_all.txt` | Updates test pinned `aiounifi` version to 90. |
| `tests/components/unifi/test_sensor.py` | Adds a WAN-status sensor test using a gateway device payload and websocket updates. |
</details>
CI is failing. Please setup a dev environment so you can catch errors locally.

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the Mealie integration’s pinned `aiomealie` dependency to v1.2.3 and refreshes Mealie test fixtures/snapshots to reflect the upstream library’s new base recipe fields now included in service and diagnostics responses.

**Changes:**
- Bump `aiomealie` from 1.2.2 to 1.2.3 in the integration manifest and generated requirements files.
- Update Mealie test fixtures to include the new recipe fields returned by the library (`recipeServings`, `recipeYieldQuantity`).
- Regenerate/adjust snapshot expectations for service and diagnostics outputs to include the new fields.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/mealie/manifest.json` | Pins Mealie integration dependency to `aiomealie==1.2.3`. |
| `requirements_all.txt` | Updates the global requirements set to `aiomealie==1.2.3`. |
| `requirements_test_all.txt` | Updates the test requirements set to `aiomealie==1.2.3`. |
| `tests/components/mealie/fixtures/get_recipe.json` | Adds new recipe fields to the mocked recipe response. |
| `tests/components/mealie/fixtures/get_mealplans.json` | Adds new recipe fields to mocked mealplan recipe payloads. |
| `tests/components/mealie/snapshots/test_services.ambr` | Updates service response snapshots to include new recipe fields. |
| `tests/components/mealie/snapshots/test_diagnostics.ambr` | Updates diagnostics snapshots to include new recipe fields. |
</details>
Thanks @andrew-codechimp

It seems to me that `get_recipies.json` and `get_mealplan_today.json` should probably also get updated to include `recipeServings` and `recipeYieldQuantity`. I could be wrong though. Could you look into that?

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes lingering tasks and timers in the `test_update_locks` test by properly cleaning up resources at the end of the test. This aligns with the broader effort in PR #166758 to fix asyncio loop scopes for pytest fixtures, which can leave hanging tasks/timers when cleanup is incomplete.

**Changes:**
- Capture the return value (unsubscribe callback) from `async_add_listener()` instead of discarding it
- Call both the cleanup callback and `async_shutdown()` at the end of the test to ensure all timers and listeners are properly removed

commits.message: Fix lingering tasks in update_coordinator test

title: Fix ToDo List Intents item casing
body:
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
When using intents to add items to lists, the items are added in the case the item appears in the intent. When using voice, that is always lowercase. If an item is added manually to a list with a different casing than lowercase, removing the item via the intent would not be able to match the item. This change capitalises items being added to lists and will match items in the list regardless of case.

Example:
```
"add apples to the shopping list"
will now add an item "Apples" to the list.
```

## Type of change
<!--

Hi @djcode

It seems you haven't yet signed a CLA. Please do so [here](https://home-assistant.io/developers/cla_sign_start/?pr=home-assistant/core#160177).

Once you do that we will be able to review and accept this pull request.

Thanks!
Please add tests that assert the new behavior.
You mention Title Case in the PR description. Is that still the case? If so, most strings in Home Assistant are Sentence case, so I think it would make sense to keep it like that

## Pull request overview

This PR adjusts the To-do intent handling so items added via intents are stored with capitalized casing, and begins to make intent matching case-insensitive to better handle voice-input lowercasing.

**Changes:**
- Capitalize the `item` slot value when handling To-do intents.
- Update add-item intent tests to expect capitalized summaries.
- Update complete-item matching to use case-insensitive comparison for summaries.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/todo/intent.py` | Capitalizes incoming intent item text and adjusts matching logic for completing items. |
| `tests/components/todo/test_intent.py` | Updates add-item intent assertions for the new capitalization behavior. |
LGTM, thanks @djcode 👍

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds fan speed percentage control to the SwitchBot Air Purifier integration. The main changes include adding the `SET_SPEED` feature to the air purifier's supported features, implementing the `percentage` property, and adding the `async_set_percentage` method. The PR also renames test constants from PMxx/VOC-based names to region-based (US/JP) names for better clarity, fixes a missing `.value` attribute on the `EVAPORATIVE_HUMIDIFIER` model in the model mappings, and adds new constant sets for grouping air purifier models.

**Changes:**
- Added fan speed percentage control to `SwitchBotAirPurifierEntity` class with `FanEntityFeature.SET_SPEED` support
- Renamed test service info constants from `AIR_PURIFIER_PM25_SERVICE_INFO`/`AIR_PURIFIER_VOC_SERVICE_INFO` to region-based names (`US`/`JP`)
- Fixed missing `.value` attribute on `SwitchbotModel.EVAPORATIVE_HUMIDIFIER` in model mappings
- Added helper constant sets for air purifier models (`AIRPURIFIER_BASIC_MODELS`, `AIRPURIFIER_TABLE_MODELS`, `AIRPURIFIER_PM25_MODELS`)
- Added test coverage for the new `set_percentage` service on air purifiers

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/switchbot/fan.py | Added `percentage` property and `async_set_percentage` method to air purifier entity, added `FanEntityFeature.SET_SPEED` to supported features |
| homeassistant/components/switchbot/const.py | Added three constant sets for air purifier model grouping |
| homeassistant/components/switchbot/__init__.py | Fixed `.value` attribute access on `EVAPORATIVE_HUMIDIFIER` model in two places |
| tests/components/switchbot/__init__.py | Renamed test constants from region-agnostic names (PM25/VOC) to region-specific names (US/JP) |
| tests/components/switchbot/test_init.py | Updated imports and us
@zerzhang Please look at the copilot findings and comment on them. Please mark it as ready for review afterwards.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes lingering async tasks in Nest integration tests by adding a new fixture that mocks the `GoogleNestSubscriber._async_run_refresh` method. This fixture is then applied to multiple test functions in the test_config_flow.py file to prevent the refresh task from running during tests and leaving hanging async operations.

**Changes:**
- Added `mock_subscriber_refresh` fixture in conftest.py that patches the subscriber refresh method to prevent lingering background tasks
- Applied the new fixture to 13 test functions in test_config_flow.py using `@pytest.mark.usefixtures` decorator

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/components/nest/conftest.py | Adds new fixture to mock async refresh operations |
| tests/components/nest/test_config_flow.py | Applies the new fixture to multiple test functions |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
See the bot review comment above.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
Thanks!

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds cleanup calls to debouncer test functions to fix lingering tasks in the test suite. The changes ensure that debouncer timer tasks are properly cancelled and references are cleaned up at the end of each test, which is particularly important for the async event loop scope fixes mentioned in PR #166758.

**Changes:**
- Added `debouncer.async_shutdown()` calls at the end of 7 test functions to properly clean up pending timer tasks and release function references
- Replaced `debouncer.async_cancel()` with `debouncer.async_shutdown()` in 2 test functions to provide more thorough cleanup
Thanks!

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a small convenience API on `ConfigEntry` to retrieve only the subentries of a given `subentry_type`, aiming to reduce future brittleness in integrations that currently assume a single subentry type.

**Changes:**
- Add `ConfigEntry.subentries_for_type(subentry_type: str) -> list[ConfigSubentry]` helper.
- Add a unit test covering basic filtering behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/config_entries.py` | Introduces the new `subentries_for_type` helper to filter `entry.subentries` by `subentry_type`. |
| `tests/test_config_entries.py` | Adds a test exercising `subentries_for_type` against multiple subentries/types. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
LGTM, thanks @joostlek 👍

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
