title: Hassfest requirements.py optimization
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Breaking change
<!--
  If your PR contains a breaking change for existing users, it is important
  to tell them what breaks, how to make it work again and why we did this.
  This piece of text is published with the release notes, so it helps if you
  write it towards our users, not us.
  Note: Remove this section if this PR is NOT a breaking change.
-->


## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
Whilst hunting some optimization patterns and running cProfile, on various parts in hassgest, I noticed the [metadata()](https://docs.python.org/3/library/importlib.metadata.html) was called 10K+ in requirements.py. Normally this is a rather static value once loaded (it reads the package data from the environment), so I wondered why it hit so much. It's because it's in the `while_to_check`-loop.
I'd figured a cache could be justified in this situation. This drastically declines the metadata-hits, to around 1.4k hits, the rest is cached.

It saved around 4 seconds in running this validation: from 9 to 5 seconds, on average.

## Type of change

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adjusts `script/hassfest/requirements.py` to cache `importlib.metadata.metadata()` lookups during requirement validation (notably the `Requires-Python` check), as part of reverting a previous “generate translations optimization” change.

**Changes:**
- Add a cached wrapper around `importlib.metadata.metadata()` inside `get_requirements()`.
- Switch the `Requires-Python` lookup to use the cached wrapper.

Nice time savings!

It's probably not worth the trouble, but is there a way to make sure the cache gets cleaned up after the loop is over?
Thanks!

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the arcam-fmj dependency from version 1.8.2 to 1.8.3 to fix a critical bug with color space handling that was causing the Arcam FMJ integration to fail in Home Assistant 2026.4.0. The upstream library release (1.8.3) addresses an `IndexError` that occurred when processing incoming video parameters with certain color space values.

**Changes:**
- Updated arcam-fmj dependency from 1.8.2 to 1.8.3 in requirements_all.txt
- Updated arcam-fmj dependency from 1.8.2 to 1.8.3 in requirements_test_all.txt
- Updated arcam-fmj dependency from 1.8.2 to 1.8.3 in the manifest.json

title: Fix Ring snapshots
body:
Adopted HusainCode’s abandoned PR #156905 with Ring camera changes.
https://github.com/home-assistant/core/pull/156905/changes#diff-c304df53986ffb88fb1f08ba914fe0d2a26fdc8bc1c2277cefae0cae56247b5d
- Updated Ring camera integration
- Copied files from abandoned branch
- Changes copied from original PR
- CI will run tests on dev

## Breaking change
n/a


## Proposed change
Fixes bug with Ring camera snapshot extraction by differentiating between live_view and last_recording camera types.

Live view cameras now fetch fresh snapshots directly from the device using async_get_snapshot().

Last recording cameras continue to extract frames from cached video recordings using ffmpeg.

Key Changes

Added conditional logic in async_camera_image() to handle live_view cameras separately.

Introduced a new _async_get_fresh_snapshot() method to fetch live snapshots.

Split the original test into two separate tests to ensure coverage for both camera types.

homeassistant/components/ring/camera.py 
Added conditional logic to differentiate snapshot retrieval methods for live_view vs last_recording cameras, and added a new method to fetch fresh snapshots.

tests/components/ring/test_camera.py
Split the original test into two separate tests: one for live_view camera snapshots and one for last_recording video frame extraction.

<!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the Ring camera integration to fix snapshot handling by separating behavior for `live_view` cameras (fresh device snapshot) versus `last_recording` cameras (frame extracted from the most recent recording).

**Changes:**
- Updated `async_camera_image()` to fetch a fresh snapshot for `live_view` cameras via `async_get_snapshot()`.
- Kept `last_recording` snapshot extraction via ffmpeg, using cached recording URLs/frames when available.
- Split the previous snapshot test into separate tests for `live_view` and `last_recording`.

title: Add pre-commit hook for copilot instructions
body:
## Breaking change
None.

## Proposed change
Add `gen_copilot_instructions` to pre-commit so changes to `AGENTS.md`, relevant skill files, the generator script, or `.github/copilot-instructions.md` regenerate the Copilot instructions before commit.

The dedicated CI validation job stays in place, matching the existing `gen_requirements_all` pattern in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`.

Running this through `prek` is still preferred as an additional safeguard because it catches the drift earlier for contributors, instead of waiting for CI to fail with `Please run: python -m script.gen_copilot_instructions`.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New integration (thank you!)

- [ ] Deprecation (breaking change to happen in the future)

## Pull request overview

Adds a local `pre-commit` safeguard to keep `.github/copilot-instructions.md` in sync with its sources, mirroring the existing pattern used for `gen_requirements_all`.

**Changes:**
- Add a `gen_copilot_instructions` local pre-commit hook that runs `python3 -m script.gen_copilot_instructions`.
- Trigger regeneration when `AGENTS.md`, skill `SKILL.md` files, the generator script, or the generated output changes.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 1 out of 1 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
I am not sure we should add this to pre-commit. Its not something that is needed for the majority of commits, while making pre-commit take even more time (even if not much).

commits.message: Add pre-commit hook for copilot instructions
Avoid no-op copilot hook runs for excluded skills

title: Fix SMHI
body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Breaking change
<!--
  If your PR contains a breaking change for existing users, it is important
  to tell them what breaks, how to make it work again and why we did this.
  This piece of text is published with the release notes, so it helps if you
  write it towards our users, not us.
  Note: Remove this section if this PR is NOT a breaking change.
-->
As the API has changed, so has also the possible values for the `Precipitation category` sensor. Any automation or other things depending on the state of this sensor needs to be adjusted accordingly.

## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
Updates the library for the `SMHI` integration to use the new API as since yesterday, the integration was in a broken state (old API turned off).

Meanwhile it's a minor breaking change on one sensor, I'd like to argue to merge and release this PR in the next patch release as that sensor isn't one that you would typically use anyway for automations/scripts and the integration itself is broken until this PR has been released.

https://github.com/gjohansson-ST/pysmhi/releases/tag/v2.0.0

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates Home Assistant’s test suite fixtures to ensure pytest-asyncio applies the correct (function-scoped) event loop to autouse fixtures, preventing them from unintentionally running against a session-scoped loop.

**Changes:**
- Switch two autouse fixtures in `tests/conftest.py` from `@pytest.fixture` to `@pytest_asyncio.fixture` so they run under the intended event loop context.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 1 out of 1 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**tests/components/heos/conftest.py:92**
* The `controller_fixture` is defined as async but uses `@pytest.fixture` decorator instead of `@pytest_asyncio.fixture`. This change contradicts the PR's goal to fix asyncio loop scopes. Async fixtures must use `@pytest_asyncio.fixture` to ensure they run with the correct event loop scope, especially for autouse fixtures.
```
@pytest.fixture(name="controller", autouse=True)
async def controller_fixture(
    players: dict[int, HeosPlayer],
    favorites: dict[int, MediaItem],
    input_sources: list[MediaItem],
    playlists: list[MediaItem],
    change_data: PlayerUpdateResult,
    group: dict[int, HeosGroup],
    quick_selects: dict[int, str],
) -> MockHeos:
```
</details>
Thanks!

commits.message: Fix asyncio loop scopes for pytest fixtures

## Pull request overview

Updates Home Assistant’s CODEOWNERS configuration to ensure agent-related configuration files/directories route review requests to the core team, aligning the generated hassfest source with the committed CODEOWNERS file.

**Changes:**
- Add CODEOWNERS entries for agent configuration docs (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md).
- Add CODEOWNERS entries for agent configuration directories (`/.agent/`, `/.claude/`, `/.gemini/`).
- Keep `script/hassfest/codeowners.py` and `CODEOWNERS` in sync so hassfest validation/generation remains consistent.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `script/hassfest/codeowners.py` | Extends the hassfest-generated CODEOWNERS base block with agent configuration ownership entries. |
| `CODEOWNERS` | Adds the corresponding generated ownership rules for agent configuration files/directories. |
Thanks!

## Pull request overview

This PR adds millihertz (`mHz`) to the documented list of supported frequency measurement units in the sensor and number device class definitions. The change reflects the fact that `UnitOfFrequency.MILLIHERTZ` is already available in the codebase and should be documented as a valid unit option.

**Changes:**
- Updated `FREQUENCY` device class documentation in sensor component to include `mHz` as a supported unit
- Updated `FREQUENCY` device class documentation in number component to include `mHz` as a supported unit

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| homeassistant/components/sensor/const.py | Added `mHz` to the documented list of supported frequency units |
| homeassistant/components/number/const.py | Added `mHz` to the documented list of supported frequency units |
Thanks @32u-nd!

reviews.body:
Hi @32u-nd

It seems you haven't yet signed a CLA. Please do so [here](https://home-assistant.io/developers/cla_sign_start/?pr=home-assistant/core#167087).

Once you do that we will be able to review and accept this pull request.

Thanks!

## Pull request overview

This pull request adds millihertz (mHz) to the `UnitOfFrequency` enum in Home Assistant's constants file. This unit is needed by the OpenEMS integration to support meters that measure net frequency in millihertz. The change is simple and follows the existing patterns in the codebase for adding sub-unit frequency measurements.

**Changes:**
- Added `MILLIHERTZ = "mHz"` enum member to the `UnitOfFrequency` class, positioned as the smallest unit before HERTZ
Test failures unrelated. Looks good to me. Thanks @32u-nd

  pace of pull request reviews will go up and your wait time will go down.
  
  When picking a pull request to review, try to choose one that hasn't yet
  been reviewed.

  Thanks for helping out!
-->

To help with the load of incoming pull requests:

- [ ] I have reviewed two other [open pull requests][prs] in this repository.

[prs]: https://github.com/home-assistant/core/pulls?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Apr+-author%3A%40me+-draft%3Atrue+-label%3Awaiting-for-upstream+sort%3Acreated-desc+review%3Anone+-status%3Afailure

author: PeteRager

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes a Sonos media metadata edge case where a single-whitespace track title causes the entity to show `paused` instead of `idle` when no media is actually present.

**Changes:**
- Normalize Sonos track titles by stripping whitespace and treating empty/whitespace-only titles as `None`.
- Add parametrized test coverage to ensure `PAUSED_PLAYBACK`/`STOPPED` map to `idle` when the title is missing or whitespace.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `homeassistant/components/sonos/media.py` | Strips the reported title and coerces empty/whitespace-only titles to `None` so state calculation can correctly return `idle`. |
| `tests/components/sonos/test_media_player.py` | Adds tests validating the `paused` vs `idle` behavior for `PAUSED_PLAYBACK` and `STOPPED` based on title presence/whitespace. |
Thanks @PeteRager !
